title: Failure to parse simulations from the newest GROMACS version
text: [nomad-lab.eu](https://nomad-lab.eu/) fails to parse my uploaded GROMACS 2024.2 simulations ([Link to GROMACS parser](https://github.com/nomad-coe/atomistic-parsers/blob/develop/atomisticparsers/gromacs/parser.py)) due to an error with MDAnalysis which is used to parse `.tpr` files (see attached screenshot).

title: Failure to parse simulations from the newest GROMACS version
text: The problem is, that the used MDAnalysis version does not support the most recent `tpr`/`tpx` file format. However, since the most recent version of MDAnalysis seems to support it ([see here](https://github.com/MDAnalysis/mdanalysis/blob/489905f613b39f6a546217867be3f3d7841294a8/package/MDAnalysis/topology/tpr/setting.py#L41)), an upgrade of the MDAnalysis library would probably solve the problem.

title: Failure to parse simulations from the newest GROMACS version
text: Hi @Luis_Walter and thanks for the report!

title: Failure to parse simulations from the newest GROMACS version
Failure to parse simulations from the newest GROMACS version
text: Unfortunately this does not yet support Gromacs 2024.

title: Failure to parse simulations from the newest GROMACS version
text:
This means that I will have to disable some features. I don't think this will affect you too much for the paper, off the top of my head it means: rdfs, msds, rgs will not be calculated, ff interactions will not be stored (although as I said before these are incomplete anyway, so I wouldn't take this into account)

Hopefully there are no other side effects, we will have to test it. Is it out of the question to repeat the calculations with the previous version of Gromacs?
short follow up -- indeed this now causes an issue with the system visualization 😮‍💨
I can reach out to the MDAnalysis devs to see what their plans are for this, but I think we should rerun with Gromacs 2023 for the paper (this is what Tristan used for his calculations)
I think the MDAnalysis Code on Github already supports tpx version 133 (that's what I linked in the initial issue message), but it is not part of the current latest release from December 2023. Rerunning everything us possible, but takes some time.
oh i see, in any case we need to wait for a stable version to have robust usage and testing, so this will not work for us for the time being

talk to Tristan about it and we can meet maybe next week if we need to discuss together
Thanks, @Joseph_Rudzinski, sounds like we should indeed avoid Gromacs 2024 for now
Np, sorry to deliver the bad news 😔

text: I encountered this on dev distro when `uv sync` updated some code.

text:
The error pops when starting the nomad appworker and looks like

```sh
Poe => nomad admin run appworker
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
    from .ui import App
  File "/home/sarthak-kapoor/repositories/fairmat/dev_distro/packages/nomad-FAIR/nomad/config/models/ui.py", line 816, in <module>
    class Menu(MenuItem):
  File "pydantic/main.py", line 292, in pydantic.main.ModelMetaclass.__new__
  File "pydantic/main.py", line 813, in pydantic.main.BaseModel.__try_update_forward_refs__
  File "pydantic/typing.py", line 566, in pydantic.typing.update_model_forward_refs
    raise TypeError(f"{self} is not subscriptable")
  File "pydantic/typing.py", line 541, in pydantic.typing.update_field_forward_refs
    checks. File "pydantic/typing.py", line 541, in pydantic.typing.update_field_forward_refs
    checks. File "pydantic/typing.py", line 532, in pydantic.typing.update_field_forward_refs
    class Any(metaclass=_AnyMeta):
  File "pydantic/typing.py", line 75, in pydantic.typing.evaluate_forwardref

title: GET request on processing upload corrupts it
text: if I send a GET request for an upload that is still processing, it corrupts it.

title: GET request on processing upload corrupts it
text: Has anyone been able to reproduce this?

title: GET request on processing upload corrupts it
text: You need to POST the upload, receive the upload_id, and immediately send a GET request for that upload id. Right now I need to have my code wait a few seconds, hope that the processing is done, and then query it.

title: GET request on processing upload corrupts it
text: But because I don't know how long the processing will take, this can be problematic.

title: GET request on processing upload corrupts it
text: An example of this can be found here: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/test/gui/user/uploads/upload/id/q-HvqWzAR8WIfkdai1z2Nw/entry/id/sZZ-mSvZsUqTumsz5M-iJrQWxYSs.

title: GET request on processing upload corrupts it
text: One of the entries failed, and there are also errors in the log (the problem compounds with the other issue I posted yesterday with publishing entries).

title: GET request on processing upload corrupts it
text:
That does not happen if I wait for processing before querying
on a side note: you can check the status of a process through the API

```python
response = requests.post(f'{url}/uploads/{upload_id}/action/process', headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'})
while True:
    time.sleep(2)
    response = requests.get(f'{url}/uploads/{upload_id}', headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'})
    if not response.json()["data"]["process_running"]:
        break
```
That's exactly the GET request I use to query the upload.

title: GET request on processing upload corrupts it
text:
Without `sleep` you will likely corrupt your upload
ah ok and this breaks with sleep?
if you sleep long enough to allow the processing to finish, it works. The problem is, how long is long enough? 🙂
ah ok i see. if this request is already raising issues then we have a problem. 2 secs usually did it for me, this never crashed so far.
ok, so I'm indeed enforcing a 2s sleep time in my code now. Maybe that's enough and won't corrupt the upload even if the processing is not quite finished? That would work for me, I wonder if there's a way to make it more foolproof, like slighlty delaying the initial POST response?
i think even 0.5s would be fine, but yeah that could also be a feature request
ok, good to know.

title: GPAW Parser Error: unsupported operand type(s) for *: 'Quantity' and 'NoneType'

title: GPAW Parser Error: unsupported operand type(s) for *: 'Quantity' and 'NoneType'
text:
The traceback suggests a problem with multiplying a `NoneType` with a `Quantity` during unit conversion:


```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/nomad/processing/data.py", line 1494, in parsing
    parser.parse(
  File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/nomad/parsing/parser.py", line 476, in parse
    self.mainfile_parser.parse(mainfile, archive, logger)
  File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/electronicparsers/gpaw/parser.py", line 665, in parse
    self.parse_method()
  File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/electronicparsers/gpaw/parser.py", line 459, in parse_method
    sec_scf.threshold_energy_change = self.apply_unit(
                                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/electronicparsers/gpaw/parser.py", line 377, in apply_unit
    return val * unit
           ~~~~^~~~~~
  File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/pint/unit.py", line 187, in __mul__
    return self._REGISTRY.Quantity(1, self._units) * other
           ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~
TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for *: 'Quantity' and 'NoneType'

Could someone from the GPAW parser team take a look?

title: GUI does not show "repeats" subsection, visibility and order does not work.
text: I wrote a schema and parser for a process which is done on multiple samples at the same time.

title: GUI does not show "repeats" subsection, visibility and order does not work.
text:
In the "Overview" it does not show the SubSection with the Sample entries. In "Data" it shows the samples correctly, so the parsing itself works. The ordering also does not work as the figures are shown at the end, and the `time` for example is still shown together with other `"visible": False"` Quantities.

```python
class FieldCoolingEntry(PlotSection, EntryData):
    m_def = Section(
        label='Field Cooling Entry',
        a_display={
            'order': [
                'samples',
                'experiment_date',
                'figures',
            ]
        },
    )

    samples = SubSection(section_def=Sample, repeats=True) # Sample is defined somewhere else

    experiment_date = Quantity(
        type=Datetime,
        description='Experiment timestamp',
    )

    blocking_temperature = Quantity(
        type=float,
        unit='°C',
        description='Blocking temperature',
    )

    plateau_duration = Quantity(
        type=float,
        unit='minute',
        description='Plateau duration',
    )

    cooling_rate = Quantity(
        type=float,
        unit='°C / minute',
        description='Cooling rate',
    )

    time = Quantity(
        type=float,
        shape=['*'],
        unit='s',
        description='Time series',
        a_display={'visible': False},
    )

   ...

```
Here you can see that the whole entry does random things
Changed the channel name: GUI does not show "reapats" subsection, visibility and order does not work. Changed the channel name: GUI does not show "repeats" subsection, visibility and order does not work.

```python

        a_eln=dict(

                    'samples',

title: GUI does not show "repeats" subsection, visibility and order does not work.

title: GUI issues related to the `default` annotation in Schema quantities
text:
There some bugs when using the `default` annotations in quantites. For example this code snippet here:
```
class Assembly(FzGrowthStep, PlotSection, ArchiveSection):
    """
    Class autogenerated from yaml schema.
    """
    m_def = Section(a_eln=dict(overview=True))
    
    name = Quantity(
        type=str,
        description=' ',
        a_eln={'component': 'StringEditQuantity'},
        default='Assembly',
    )
```
will look like this in the `data` section: see scrennshot1

However, the the `default="Assembly"` is not picked up as the step name in the parent section. It only shows a the index number. And on the `Overview` section it appears empty: see scrennshot2

Also the Section is named by the parent class `FzGrowthStep` instead of `Assembly`. The same problems shows up in an search app for another schema. When `default` is used those quantities result in empty columns in the search table.

Like when I make a Quantity(type=HDFReference), can I in my schema fix that whenever Nomad writes this data it directs it to HDF5 in my specfied path? So, there are 2 types:
1. `HDF5Reference` points to a specific dataset within a hdf5 filepath you have to pass when parsing / reading.
2. `HDF5Dataset` stores the passed data in an hdf5 generated in the archive. So it does like a combined effect of generate a hdf5 file + then add the hdf5 reference to that dataset.

so just visualization?

there is also a basesection `HDF5Normalizer` that you can inherit/use in your custom schema. You can deop your h5 file, define path to the sections of your hdf5 file with proper quantity annotations.

title: Help with parse_section()
text:
I am trying to use `parse_section()` of the `Parser(ABC)` class --> `file_parser.py`, as I have done often in the past. However, I am working with a new schema, so perhaps this is causing my difficulties:

Here is the code for `parse_section()`:

```python
def parse_section(self, data: Dict[str, Any], root: MSection) -> None:
    for key, val in data.items():
        if not hasattr(root, key):
            continue

        section = getattr(root.m_def.section_cls, key)
        if isinstance(section, SubSection):
            for val_n in [val] if isinstance(val, dict) else val:
                sub_section = section.sub_section.section_cls()
                root.m_add_sub_section(section, sub_section)
                self.parse_section(val_n, sub_section)
            continue

        root.m_set(root.m_get_quantity_definition(key), val)
```

I am passing the following dictionary to `parse_section()` in my test:

```
{'step': 0, 'total_energy': {'value': <Quantity(0.0, 'joule')>}
```

The schema for the root section which I am passing looks something like (I am simplifing here by extracting some inheritance directly into the class so it is visible):

```python
class TrajectoryOutputs(WorkflowOutputs):

    step = Quantity(
        type=np.int32,
        description="""
        The step number with respect to the workflow.
        """,
    )

    total_energy = SubSection(SubSection=TotalEnergy.m_def, repeats=True)

class TotalEnergy(PhysicalProperty):
    """
    Section containing the total energy of a (sub)system.
    """

    value = Quantity(
        type=np.float64,
        unit='joule',
        description="""
        The value of the total energy.
        """,
```
nb - `PhysicalProperty` is an `ArchiveSection`

The quantity `step` is parsed correctly, but when `parse_section` goes to parse `total_energy` I have the issue that the `total_energy` subsection does not have an attribute called subsection, i.e., using the variables as defined in `parse_section`:

`root` = TrajectoryOutputs(total_energy, step)
`root.m_def` = nomad_simulations.outputs.TrajectoryOutputs:Section
`section` = nomad_simulations.outputs.Outputs.total_energy:SubSection
`section.m_def` = nomad.metainfo.metainfo.SubSection:Section
`section.subsection` = None

Can anyone give me some direction as to what might be going wrong here?
`TrajectoryOutputs.total_energy` is having a wrong attribute def.

title: Help with parse_section()
text:
It should be:
```python
class TrajectoryOutputs(WorkflowOutputs):

    total_energy = SubSection(sub_section=TotalEnergy.m_def, repeats=True)
```

and not:
```python
class TrajectoryOutputs(WorkflowOutputs):

    total_energy = SubSection(SubSection=TotalEnergy.m_def, repeats=True)
```

(note the `sub_section` and `SubSection`)
You can also use `section_def` without the need of adding `m_def`, something like:
```python
    total_energy = SubSection(section_def=TotalEnergy, repeats=True)
```
🤦‍♂️ Thanks @ChemaP I knew it was something stupid! Is it preferred not to use m_def?
no prob, I just came from taking with an user and the problem was the same! we just spotted it, so I had it fresh
both are equivalent, so I think you can use whatever feels more comfortable, but probably Theodore knows more

ERROR    nomad.app            [REDACTED_PHONE]-25T10:24:28 unexpected exception in API
  - exception:   + Exception Group Traceback (most recent call last):
... File "/home/femtolab_admin/nomad/nomad-distro-dev/packages/nomad-FAIR/nomad/app/v1/routers/entries.py", line 488, in perform_search

                          ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

        aggregations, histogram_responses, bucket_values = _buckets_to_interval(
                                                           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
      File "/home/femtolab_admin/nomad/nomad-distro-dev/packages/nomad-FAIR/nomad/search.py", line 1685, in _buckets_to_interval
        histogram_responses[agg_name] = HistogramAggregation(
                                        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
      File "/home/femtolab_admin/nomad/nomad-distro-dev/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/pydantic/main.py", line 214, in __init__

    pydantic_core._pydantic_core.ValidationError: 1 validation error for HistogramAggregation

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text: I'm trying to describe some capabilities of various lab instruments in a nomad schema packages. In our lab we would like for example to describe the range of values available, for example of pressure in a process chamber.

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text:
The current solution is to design the quantities for the min and max value and then searching them in the research app. In this way if an user will search for an equipment that works with an x value of pressure, it can be searched by imposing the mimum values of the entries less or equal to the value desired and the maximum value greater or equal to x. I hope that this could works but there is a more refined way to deal with this kind of problems in NOMAD?
@Hampus_Näsström This reminds me of the discussion about PhysicalProperty but I don't know what the current state is and where this can be found.

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text: Can you say more?

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text: Or who could we ask?

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text: I think a min and max quantity sounds like a great solution here.

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text: This reminds me also of the request for a ranges search widget we discussed at some point, e.

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text: G.

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text: To find all datasets covering a certain range of values in one property, e.

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text: G.

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text:
An axis range this would be quite helpful, currently I have a similar workaround with two search widgets for min and max values
In a scatterplot it is possible, If I understand what you are trying to do correctly.

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text:
Such filtering also works with histograms, the issue is more that it is about ranges covered within a specific experiment, e.g. the energy range scanned in an experiment. The goal (at least for my case) would be to find all experiments which have data within a certain energy window, where each experiment has a range of energies. One can search for min and max of the energy range individually (and combining two searches also achieve the required), but not in one search widget. This is what in my view such a range search widget should do, i.e. search whether the selected range is inside the span (min->max). Not sure if this is easily doable in elasticsearch, though
This is exactly the point.

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text: In my case I have some instruments that should be findable by a research app.

title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
text:
Here I'm trying to describe capabilities, i.e. range of values of operative parameters allowed by the instrument. In this way if I would operate some experiment with a certain value of energy, current or any kind of parameters I can search all the instruments which contains that value in the range of allowed ones. Clearly a single value or a range of values contained in the range described (the exact value could be considered as an interval of zero length)
We can't index array values in elastic search as it would quickly fill up the index. So for now you should go with the `min`, `max` quantities (which you can calculate from your array and populate in the `normalize()` method). Sure, still having such a ranges widget based on these values would be good: find me all datasets where a certain value or range falls within the span min-max
Yes, but we will have to store the min and max values as quantities to do this

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text:
Dear all,

I've been struggling in the last few days with updating my local oasis to latest nomad + having some local patches on top.

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text: I already received some help from @ahmed_ilyas which I'm really grateful for, however I think I did not make my intentions super clear originally, so I'll try to describe in more detail what is my setup. What I'm trying to do some machine learning based on the upstream NOMAD data. I want to combine results from several DFT codes and I'm really happy with NOMAD here since the metainfo and parser support are great to mostly enable this. Specifically, what I need are some results, like structures, energies, or forces, but also quite detailed info about the method (used exchange-correlation functional, whether some more advanced stuff was used, like vdW, +U, charged cell, spin-polarization, spin-orbit,  etc.) and numerical settings (basis set size, k-point density, convergence, ...) to be able to create a consistent dataset with reasonable accuracy. There are some missing pieces so I need to make some local changes to parsers and also nomad-lab itself (datamodel, normalizing, etc.) deploy it locally in my oasis (running in Kubernetes cluster of my university), download the relevant raw data from upstream (approx. tens of thousands of entries) and reprocess it using my custom setup. The recommended way to customize Oasis is nomad-distro-template, which is simple and allows to customize the plugins. I cannot however customize nomad-lab itself (unless I actually manage to build a local package of it first with the GUI, which I did not yet), so this is limited to a release version only (while some of the local patches are probably suitable for upstream, some probably don't make sense beyond my custom oasis and in general to be able to work efficiently I can't wait for a release to be able to update my oasis. I was also suggested nomad-distro-dev which allows to setup a really custom environment and submodules are something I'm already familiar with. But I can't build a docker image from this, so not really suitable for running of oasis in K8s.

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text: I'm aware that not everything I'm doing is supported (custom nomad-lab patches) or K8s nomad installations.

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text: However from my point of view this is a regression, since in the past it was possible to have a single working tree with submodules, install it locally, run quick testing with bare-bone linux while still being able to build a docker image when needed using the exact same environment.

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text: Any advice would be welcomed.

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text: Thanks for the detailed issue.

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text:
I can try to answer some of the technical stuff, the physics stuff is a bit beyond my scope but @Sarthak_Kapoor might find the ML use case interesting and see if NOMAD actions might be a fit. This is a work in progress, but you can take a look at the docs and see if this could be something that might work here: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-docs/pull/52 

> I cannot however customize nomad-lab itself. This is a limitation by design. In order for the plugin system to function reliably and allow plugins to interoperate across different installations, the base `nomad-lab` must remain compatible and consistent.

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text: In the past, we encountered significant issues with customized nomad versions.

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text: Certain plugins would work in one setup but break in another.

text:
Potential solutions to revert back previous behaviour: 
1.

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text:
In your nomad-lab branch, bring back the Dockerfile final stages from before and build it locally the previous way.
2.

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text: Create a fork of nomad-lab and release a package under a different name and make nomad-distro-template install this custom package instead of nomad-lab.

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text: Neither of the solutions are optimal but it could work.

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text: Thanks, I'll try the option 1 and report how it goes.

text: Hi @Pavel_Ondračka, your use case is quite interesting.

title: How to run oasis with custom nomad-lab patches?
text: We are building a feature that allows triggering long-running tasks in NOMAD, independently from the entry processing, and provides quite some freedom in what resources you wanna allocate for these tasks.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text:
Dear NOMAD Team,
when writing a search app I can include filters like this:
```python
schema = (
    'nomad_pvcomb.schema_packages.pvcomb_schema_package.PVcomB_JVmeasurement_LEDSoSim'
)

app = App(
    filters=Filters(include=[f'*#{schema}']),
    filters_locked={'section_defs.definition_qualified_name': schema},
)
```
This works fine.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text: In our plugin we have several JV schemas from different setups.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text: They all inherit from a common baseclass.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text: If I try to set this common baseclass as a filter and try to use an attribute it gives an error that the definition could not be found and that I should remove it from the search.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text:
Is there any way to use the baseclasses instead of the schema classes? Otherwise this would mean that I need to have an app for every schema, at least if I want to narrow down the results by filters and if I want to use common widgets. I know that for the most important JV values I could use the results. But not all desired values are in there and also the same applies for some wet chemical processes so I would be happy if there is any other way. Best regards,
Matthias
I would be also interested in an option to have multiple (closely related) schemas in one app.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text: Hi hi!

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text: We are working on two things.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text: Ideally we would be able to pick definitions in an app from a parent class. Eventually, the new guy will allow you to pick any quantity without having to have them registered in bthe app.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text:

```python
schemas = [

# noqa: E501

    label='Halide Perovskite Ions Database',
    path='perovskite-ions',
    category='Solar cells',
    description='Search ions used in halide perovskites compunds',
    search_quantities=SearchQuantities(include=schemas),
...
```

        'results.eln.sections': ['PerovskiteAIon', 'PerovskiteBIon', 'PerovskiteXIon']
    },
```
Hi @Pepe_Marquez , thanks I didn't try to use the results.eln.sections as a list in filters_locked but I tried with the section defs.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text: Scatter)?

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text: What is needed is (for example) if I have 3 very similar etch processes I want to plot etched thickness over time of all 3 processes.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text:
With a common base class available it would be easy.
@Lauri_Himanen what do you think about it. this would be a really helpful feature.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text:
I see 2 options:
1. having a wildcard/regex logic to select quantities for multiple schemas
2. adding not only the leaf schema but all parent schemas to the index
We actually are already wokring on an implementation of your option #2. We have an implementation that adds information about the full inheritance structure to our search index.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text: We are not yet sure how the actual search syntax would look like though.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text:
Our initial idea that we will enable queries like: `data<A>.section<B>.quantity = X`, where `A` and `B` can be the `m_def` of any base sections, not just the actual instance section.
imagine such a quantity in lets say a scatter plot: `data.jv_curves[*].fill_factor_in_percent#nomad_pvcomb.schema_packages.pvcomb_schema_package.PVcomB_JVmeasurement_LEDSoSim` `PVcomB_JVmeasurement_LEDSoSim` inherrits from a general JV schema. If one could exchange `nomad_pvcomb.schema_packages.pvcomb_schema_package.PVcomB_JVmeasurement_LEDSoSim` with the parent class and then also other children would be selected?

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text: This is what we would like to do. Yes, this is the idea.

title: How to use Baseclasses in Search Apps?
text:
The syntax would just look different, you would instead say:

`data<JVBaseSchema>.jv_curves[*].fill_factor_in_percent`

which makes it more explicit that you wish to specify the base section of the `data` item. Such a query would return all matching search quantities where `data` has at some level of the inheritance used `JVBaseSchema`.
ah ok would this replace the `#` syntax? We will keep the # syntax for backwards-compatibility, but yes essentially it will be a more powerful replacement of that syntax.
thank you Lauri, then +3 from our side
this sounds beautiful... thanks a lot Lauri.

Hi Sandor!

title: In and ELN lane, the rendering of API response in json is overlapping with fields.
text: Sorry, I'm answering late here.To find out why a reference to an Entity does not work in your schema, we will have to try and create a minimal non-working example.

title: In and ELN lane, the rendering of API response in json is overlapping with fields.
text:
Do you have the Python code that defines `pynxtools.Identifier.reference`? I could not spot anything wrong in this link that you sent me: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis/gui/analyze/metainfo/pynxtools/[REDACTED_EMAIL]/reference/_reference

I created a minimal example that still seems to work:

```python
from nomad.datamodel.data import Schema
from nomad.datamodel.metainfo.annotations import ELNAnnotation
from nomad.datamodel.metainfo.basesections import Entity
from nomad.metainfo import Quantity, SchemaPackage

m_package = SchemaPackage()


class Test(Schema):
    entity_reference = Quantity(
        type=Entity,
        a_eln=ELNAnnotation(
            component='ReferenceEditQuantity',
        ),
    )


m_package.__init_metainfo__()
```

If I instantiate an entry from this schema and upload the JSON file you provided (Identifier_exp3b6a73f710eaf793557ddecb76d0f73e.archive.json), I am able to succesfully populate the reference in the GUI without any errors (see screenshot below). So to debug this further, I would need to know the Python code that contains/creates the definition for: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis/gui/analyze/metainfo/pynxtools/[REDACTED_EMAIL]/reference/_reference.

text: Hello i was wondering if it is possible to generate pdfs in my parser and then parse them into my oasis so that i am able to access them later on. Is that in any way possible? If so how would my schema need to look like ?

title: Include PDF in Oasis
text: Absolutely.

title: Include PDF in Oasis
text:
You can attach they python code which generates the PDF to your parser or normalize method and use the `archive.m_context.raw_file()` context to create the file. If you want the PDFs to be searchable you can either add them as a file edit quantity in an ELN or create a simple matching parser which matches them. If you tell me a bit more about your use case I might be able to give you some advice on which way to go. In terms of python packages for handling PDFs I think there are a lot of packages out there but I've had some good experience with `PyPDF2`
Actually, I know realized that maybe you aren't talking about Portable Document Format, haha
So first question: What do you mean by PDF? 😅
hi sorry for the confusion i am looking to generate test reports which are including graphs aswell as some text which i want to store in a .pdf file format. I want this files to be viewable and also downloadable for researchers
I dont want to read values from a pdf and then store them in the oasis but i want to store the pdf itselfe in the oasis so that i would be able to access/download it
Absolutely, you can definitely do this. @Micha used to do this for experimental plans, but perhaps this is not the case anymore?

' + str(html)) plan_obj.batch_plan_pdf = output ``` we dont use this anymore though thanks a lot i will try it out asap 😄 In the file browser, you can, of course, preview the PDF without the annotation: This will be better in the new UI 🙏 So I implemented it now and see the issue with the pdf since one is only able to pass strings with the .write functions therefore i also transitioned to html but with that i have the issue that with the RawFileAdaptor i only get a preview of the code i wrote but not the rendered page. I just saw that you can use HtmlValue to display a string as html page but then i need to have two seperate variables one for the file and the other for the preview whihc is not memory efficient at all. Is there a way that the html code can be previewed as an image

title: Include new modifications of plugins in the Oasis Docker image
text: I am working on plugins for an Oasis with two separate repositories, one for the plugin and one for the image distribution. The two repositories were made using the templates (`nomad-plugin-template` and `nomad-distribution-template`) available in the FAIRmat Github. My workflow is that I am developing my parser plugin locally and testing it with the `nomad parse` command to see if the information are extracted and I then push the modifications on the repo of the plugin. To see the modification on my Oasis, I regenerate the image by triggering the Actions workflow 'Build and publish docker images' and then, `docker compose pull` the new image, as described in the README of the repo. However, when I introduce new commits in my plugin repo, these changes are not integrated when I regenerate the Docker image. In the past, I used to completely delete the caches before regenerating the image so that the modifications in the plugin were then integrated in the Docker image.

title: Include new modifications of plugins in the Oasis Docker image
text: But it seems that it is not working anymore.

title: Include new modifications of plugins in the Oasis Docker image
text: The only option I found to integrate the new version of my plugin in my Docker image was to remove my plugin from the list, commit the change, let the new image be generated and then, include back my plugin in the list with a new commit and use the new generated image. That's why I had some questions about how the content of the plugins are handled when the image is generated and how it was possible to fluidify this workflow here and easily integrate the new changes of my plugin in my Oasis image.

title: Include new modifications of plugins in the Oasis Docker image
text: Hi, are you repositories public?

title: Include new modifications of plugins in the Oasis Docker image
text: If so could you link them?

title: Include new modifications of plugins in the Oasis Docker image
text: Are you using any version tags for your plugin?

title: Include new modifications of plugins in the Oasis Docker image
text:
If so, I think simply increasing the minor version and updating the pyprojcet.toml in your oasis repo should  do the trick
If not you could also specify a commit (assuming you install it from a git repo since you can't really push new changes to PyPI without updating the version tag). I'm assuming that you now just have the plugin dependency in the Oasis pyproject.toml pointing to the main branch or something like that? The two are public yes: 
- here for the Docker image: https://github.com/Guillaume-Gaisne/New-Oasis-mechanism
- here for the parser plugin: https://github.com/Guillaume-Gaisne/nomad-TXRM_parser
I am not using any versioning for now, this is really for getting a first draft on my side before pushing functioning results in the final repos. This is the import line of my plugin in the `pyproject.toml` file: 
`  "nomad-txrm-parser @ git+https://github.com/Guillaume-Gaisne/nomad-TXRM_parser.git",`
I only have one `main` branch. But I will try with the commit hash added at the end, yeah. Yeah, try adding the git hash to the end. This will also trigger the build process so in the end it's not much extra work.

title: Inheritance of annotations
text:
Consider the following parent and child sections, where the annotations are defined in the parent section.
```py
class Parent(ArchiveSection):
  m_def = Section(
        a_eln=ELNAnnotation(
            properties=SectionProperties(
                order=[
                    'quantity1',
                    'quantity2',
                  ],
            ),
        ),
    )
    quantity1 = Quantity(type=np.float64)
    quantity3 = Quantity(type=np.float64)

class Child(Parent):
    m_def = Section()
    quantity2 = Quantity(type=np.float64)
```

It appears that when the child section is populated, it does not contain the annotations from the parent. Here's a screenshot of a similar situation, where the child section (NIRGain) misses the section annotations defined in the parent (SettingOverWavelengthRange). Can we have an inheritance of the section annotations (possibly conditioned on the child's annotation: if there aren't any, use the ones from the parent)? Currently, I have a bunch of child sections of the same parent, but I have to define the order of the quantities for each of them. Hey, I think there is a recent issue for this:
https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/2049
it is about the order right? yes this was never inherrited it needs to specified on the lowest level, you can hower change the order of the quantities in the parent class it self, sth like this:
```
quantity3 = Quantity(type=np.float64)
quantity1 = Quantity(type=np.float64)
```
i for my part copy it all in the child sections for years
it is the same with hiding quantities
looks like the things in m_def = Section() are not carried over inheriting classes
Ah interesting! i just realized this. The order of the quantities defined within a section is preserved standalone, either child or parent. Order becomes undefined when quantities from the child combine with the ones from the parent
indeed, since the annotations are also the state of the object, it will be nicer if they are also  inherited just like the quantities (possibly tracking which annotations are overwritten or inherited)
Yes but this never works.

title: Inheritance of annotations
text:
It also doesn’t work to overwrite annotations of a quantity in a child  other then restating the whole quantity
It is one of the small things you first find confusing but start to love over time 🙂
Yeah, it seems that the best way to change an inherited quantity is to completely redefine it or do sth like
```py
    name = Parent.name.m_copy()
    name.description = 'Changing the description'
```
this way the eln annotations of the quantity are also carried over
But not the a_eln annotations to my knowledge
If you change them like that
Use this with care
2 month ago I fixed bugs removing this
Yes, it seems involved. i was just trying to do this for m_def 
```py
m_def = Parent.m_def.m_copy()
m_def.description = 'something'
```
and unsurprisingly, this creates issues when overwriting a quantity. Copying the m_def of parent section as is definitely not the right thing to do for preserving the annotations 😛
So i guess we stick with redefining the annotations for the children
Yeah I think this is safer and not necessarily bad
Can help with selfdocumentation quite a lot
Yeah i agree.

title: Inheritance of annotations
text: What do you mean by self documentation though?

title: Inheritance of annotations
text:
Ah that you don’t need to inverse jump through the full hierarchy to understand the schema
Imagine 6 class inheritances each changing a bit of a quantity.

title: Inheritance of annotations
text:
Then debugging it
Considering this I might be ok with how it is^^
ah yeah! in that case, it does make sense to stick with the current way of working! 🤝

title: Is the shape of a Quantity preserved when overwriting?

title: Is the shape of a Quantity preserved when overwriting?
text:
I've created my own Power class based on the `TimeSeries` in `nomad_material_processing.vapor_deposition.general` - overwriting the value, originally to have the correct units: 
```py
class Power(TimeSeries):
    value = Quantity(
        type=float,
        unit='watt',
        shape=['*'],
        a_eln=ELNAnnotation(
            component=ELNComponentEnum.NumberEditQuantity,
        ),
    )
    set_value = Quantity(
        type=float,
        unit='watt',
        # shape=['*'],
        a_eln=ELNAnnotation(
            component=ELNComponentEnum.NumberEditQuantity,
        ),
    )
```
I use this class a SubSection in another ArchiveSection: 
```py
class TargetReference(EntityReference):
  power = SubSection(section_def=Power)
```
Until here nothing unusual. 

---- 

Since working with only a single setpoint value I did go ahead and commend out the `shape=['*']` attribute of `set_value`. But still after reinstalling the package, deleting and reprocessing the entry, refreshing the browser cache etc. - I still can add more than one value to it, even though the GUI states it as overwritten attribute. Is this supposed to happen and can I stop wondering? 😅
Did you try to overwrite with `shape=[]`? maybe that would overwrite the shape. If you don't set one, I guess the original shape is still valid?

title: Is the shape of a Quantity preserved when overwriting?
text: Mh.

title: Is the shape of a Quantity preserved when overwriting?

•    Elasticsearch version: 7.17.1 (running via Docker).

title: Issue Starting Nomad Server: Connection Problems with Services

Best regards,
Rosario Forlenza
Hi!

Can you try the following to see if you're able to establish a connection with elasticsearch using the python client?

```
❯ uv run python
>>> from elasticsearch import Elasticsearch
>>> es = Elasticsearch("http://localhost:9200")
>>> indices = es.indices.get(index="_all")

```

nomad_entries_v1

So the connection to Elasticsearch seems to be working, what could be the problem then?

title: Issue Starting Nomad Server: Connection Problems with Services
text:
The url could possibly be different, in `packages/nomad-FAIR/infrastructure.py` there's a function called `setup_elastic`

Can you try printing out the auth and host?

```

def setup_elastic():
    """Creates connection to elastic search."""
    http_auth = None
    if config.elastic.username and config.elastic.password:
        http_auth = (config.elastic.username, config.elastic.password)
    global elastic_client
    print(http_auth, '%s:%d' % (config.elastic.host, config.elastic.port))
    elastic_client = connections.create_connection(
        hosts=['%s:%d' % (config.elastic.host, config.elastic.port)],
        timeout=config.elastic.timeout,
        max_retries=10,
        retry_on_timeout=True,
        http_auth=http_auth,
    )
    logger.info('setup elastic connection')
    from nomad.metainfo.elasticsearch_extension import (
        create_indices as create_v1_indices,
    )

    create_v1_indices()
    logger.info('initialized v1 elastic indices')

    return elastic_client
```
Greetings

>     if not self.elastic_client.indices.exists(index=self.index_name):
>            ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'indices'
@Lauri_Himanen do you know what might be happening here?

title: Issue Starting Nomad Server: Connection Problems with Services
text: Hi @rosario.forlenza !

title: Issue Starting Nomad Server: Connection Problems with Services
text: So I think that Elasticsearch is running fine, but could be that somehow WSL is not letting some of the connections through. I think you are following the `nomad-distro-dev` setup in which Elasticsearch is run through docker, and then the app+worker are run as a separate python process that is outside docker.

title: Issue Starting Nomad Server: Connection Problems with Services
text:
Are you using Docker desktop wih the WSL2 backend? You could try the following:
 - If `docker compose` and `uv run poe start` are run in different WSL distributions, they cannot communicate by default. You could check this by running the command `wsl -l -v` in both terminals to verify that they run in the same WSL distribution. Also you could check if you can run `docker ps` in the terminal which you use to run `uv run poe start`.

title: Issue with docker for a local installation of NOMAD

title: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration
text:
When executing NOMAD, it seems to misinterpret the unit "min" as "millinch" instead of "minute",this causes errors in the code. I've noticed that in the NOMAD Oasis, the documentation uses "docker compose" instead of "docker-compose".
hey the correct unit term for minute is `minute`,
this is intended
if you need milliinch it is min 😄
i am not sure why it is overloading `pint` here just tested with `pint`and there min resolves to minute
Thank you for the clarification!
@Astou_THIAM this was an intentional "error" to show people how they can understand and fix mistakes. This explanation is inlcuded in the docs here: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis/docs/tutorial/develop_plugin.html#run-the-nomad-cli
Not sure what you mean with the "docker compose" vs the "docker-compose"? `docker compose` is the command that should be used to run the docker image as defined in the `docker-compose.yaml` config file: https://docs.docker.com/reference/cli/docker/compose/
older version of docker uses `docker-compose` to run docker config files, but recent versions use `docker compose` command
Hi everyone!

title: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration
text:
HI Astou, I'll try to provide some answers for the two questions:
1.

title: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration
text: What do you mean by "reading a file"? Do you mean when you upload a file to NOMAD?

title: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration
text: What parser gets called is decide by the matching criteria. The simplest criteria is to match on either the file name/ending or the mime type of the file using the `MatchingParser` class.

title: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration
text:
You can find more information about this in the docs here: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/docs/explanation/processing.html#matching
2.

title: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration
text: Not sure exactly what you mean by graphs.

title: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration
text: NOMAD doesn't have a graph backend but we do have an option of finding the back references between entries.

title: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration
text: These are resolved in the GUI so to navigate in a graphical sense you can follow the references where they are place in the data section and the back references can all be found on the overview page.

title: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration
text: As far as I know we have no API endpoint for querying across multiple references, this would have to be done on the client side.

title: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration
text: Maybe if you provide some more information on what you want to do I could provide more information.

title: Issues with name of classes which are the same

I think this interests you, @Markus_Scheidgen @Theodore_Chang_DM_BITTE

Apparently, there are issues sometimes when defining classes or quantities with the same name.

title: Issues with name of classes which are the same
text: It happened to me when dealing with the `GW(ExcitedStateMethodology)` and `BSE(ExcitedStateMethodology)` method classes, both had a `type` quantity and it was a problem when using `m_copy` in results (check [these lines in the schema](https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/datamodel/results.py?ref_type=heads#L1944), and how I had to patch it).

title: Issues with name of classes which are the same
text: Now I think I have a similar issue.

title: Issues with name of classes which are the same
text:
There are two classes and they are in conflict:

1. one defined in the `schema/simulation/run`, called `Energy(MSection)` (check [this](https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-schema-plugin-run/blob/develop/runschema/calculation.py#L338)),
2. and another new one I am defining in the `schema/simulation/data`, called `Energy(Variables)` (check [this](https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-schema-plugin-simulation-data/blob/develop/src/nomad_simulations/variables.py#L112)). When a Normalizer (especifically, the [DOSNormalizer](https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-normalizer-plugin-dos/blob/develop/dosnormalizer/normalizer.py#L57)) runs for the `run.energy` section, instead of considering the point 1 section, it is considering that it is the one defined in point 2, and hence it is missing some attributes and raising an AttributeError when doing `MSection.__getattr__` in the normalizer for some specific quantity.

title: Issues with name of classes which are the same

title: Issues with referencing quantities
text:
There is something wrong with the shape of KMesh.points. Somehow it has a shape of  ['n_points'], which breaks the referencing system. You can see this difference here:

```python
from nomad_simulations.numerical_settings import KMesh as KMeshSettings
from nomad_simulations.variables import KMesh

a = KMesh()
print(a.m_def.all_quantities["points"].shape)
b = KMeshSettings(points=[[complex(0, 0)]])
# This will crash!
# a.points = b

from runschema.calculation import BandStructure as BS
from nomad.datamodel.results import BandStructure

c = BandStructure()
print(c.m_def.all_quantities["reciprocal_cell"].shape)
d = BS(reciprocal_cell=[[0, 0, 0], [0, 0, 0], [0, 0, 0]])
# This works fine!
c.reciprocal_cell = d
```
Could you check if some part of your code modifies the shape of `KMesh.points`?

text:

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: Currently, the memory on many of the oases gets blocked over time and the only way to free it is to restart the containers. @Markus_Scheidgen mentioned to me at some point that this is due to the workers being reused so if there is some memory leak in them they block the memory indefinitely. He did mention that there was a way to limit the reuse and/or number of workers somehow to mitigate this.

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: As it's Markus last day today I guess he won't be able to answer but maybe @Lauri_Himanen has an idea who could?

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: Thanks!

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text:
Probably this - https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/stable/userguide/workers.html#max-memory-per-child-setting
Cool!

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: Any idea what could be a reasonable value for this?

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: How many workers do we create?

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text:
I guess that's also configurable
Ideally the number of workers times the max memory would be less than some fraction of the system memory I guess
By default it's the number of cores https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/stable/userguide/workers.html#concurrency
Great, thanks!
`python -c "import multiprocessing; print(multiprocessing.cpu_count())"` should give you the right number
I guess the max memory per child has a similar effdect.

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: But, there is another important setting.

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: This is the one i was mentioning once.

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text:
This one specifically mentiones the use case of memory leaks: https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/latest/userguide/workers.html#max-tasks-per-child-setting
Basically allows you to restart a worker process based on the number of tasks it has run.

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: Eg. restart a worker every 100 archives.

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: Ah, right!

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: Now I remember.

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: Thank you!

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: Do we have default values for this stored somewhere?

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text:
There is nothing on the docker compose, but the k8s version of nomad uses 128
Its in the default values.yaml of the helm chart in the distro project.

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: Okay, great!

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text:
It would possibly make sense to have a smaller default for the distro template @ahmed_ilyas as a lot of people start out with not so much system memory. Yeah i think that makes sense
I am in a similar situation that I want to limit the number of workers for our oasis, as the machine has very many cores.

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text: Where and how can I configure this?

title: Limiting the Oasis workers to save memory
text:
We set the limit on number of tasks https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-distro-template/pull/85
Ah, thanks, now I know where these parameters go, thanks 🙂

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text:
After following the last tutorial on the installation of an Oasis using the Github repo to create a Docker image of an Oasis with plugin code installed, I am trying to make it work on my machine by developing a parser for the DAMASK code. Since I am not familiar with the coding convention of NOMAD, I make a lot of changes to test the output visible on the Oasis I am creating and so it means that, following the steps of the tutorial, once I make a modification on the parser code, I generate a new Docker image that I want to test. So each time I want to test my new Oasis image, I do `sudo rm -r nomad-oasis` with `nomad-oasis` being the folder containing the `docker-compose.yaml` file and the `configs` folder and I also delete the Docker image with `docker rmi <id-of-the-image>` and then I `curl` the new image and proceed to the steps of the installation. However, when I open the Oasis in my browser, I can see the entries but when I click on one of them (each of them), I have this message:
```
Unexpected error: "[object Object] (500)".

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text:
Please try again and let us know, if this error keeps happening.
```

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image

Is this a normal behaviour or am I doing something wrong when I am trying to visualize the modifications I made on my image by deleting the repo and the Docker image systematically ?

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text: Hi Guillaume!

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text:
You should not need to delete anything when updating the image. Taken directly from the image README:
"Whenever you update your image you need to shut down NOMAD using

`docker compose down`

and then repeat steps 5. and 6. above."
With steps 5. and 6. being: 
"
5.

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text:
Pull the images specified in the docker-compose.yaml (retrieved from the nomad-oasis.zip). Note that the image needs to be public or you need to provide a PAT (see "Important" note above).

`docker compose pull`

6. And run it with docker compose in detached (--detach or -d) mode

`docker compose up -d`
"
When you do the delete command from above you are deleting the files folder which contains all of the entries so that is why NOMAD cannot find them.

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text: Ok thanks !

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text: Guess I haven't checked the image README correctly...

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text:
So if the files folder has been deleted, how is it that the entries in the Explore page are still showing up ? I mean, I can still see the entries but if I go the their Entry Page, there, nothing is displayed
That's a bit above my knowledge of the NOMAD backend but my guess would be that the Mongo DB index somehow survived in it's own docker image while the files it's referencing to were deleted. @Markus_Scheidgen might be able to tell you more. It is possible see the entries and have the files deleted. Depending on the view the GUI shows information from mongodb or from elasticsearch.

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text: With our default docker-compose based install the mongo and elastic databases are put into a docker volume. If you use the same docker installation, but removed all the files, the docker volumes will still be there. If you want to delete everything, you also need to delete the docker volumes.

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text: By default the volumes are called `nomad_oasis_elastic`, `nomad_oasis_mongo` and `nomad_oasis_rabbitmq`.

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text:
With `docker volume rm ...` you can delete volumes. If you routinely want to wipe all data in your NOMAD installation, you can also use the nomad cli from within one of the containers:
```
docker exec -ti nomad_oasis_app bash
nomad admin reset --i-am-really-sure
```
If you just want to update your Oasis you should not delete anything. All uploaded data lives in the `.volumes` folder and will be lost if you delete it.

title: Link to entries broken when changing the Oasis Docker image
text: Just follow what @Hampus_Näsström was writing. `down` `pull` `up -d` again.

text: This is a tricky question.

title: Logout button does not logout north
text:
In keycloak each client (application/service) works separately even they belong to the same realms.
with admin priviledges on keycloak we can logout from all section for a given user but doing as a user it is not obvious
https://w0l1d.medium.com/implementing-global-logout-in-keycloak-a-comprehensive-guide-c6f22d4034bd
I can find single-sign out in the list of feature of keycloak (https://www.keycloak.org/docs/25.0.6/server_admin/index.html)
but I do not find any other documentation about the details or how we should set it up
So, currently you also have to log in to NOMAD and NORTH independently, right?
because the two clients belongs to the same realm you only need to login one thanks to the single sign in method.
as far as i know the when you logout from one client
you also remove the session token and you can login with another user
from the another client (application) point of view the old session token still valid and usable but it belong to another account.... Ok, so we would have to invalidate the session token so that the second app does a reauthentification. Could there be cases where people would like to stay logged into NORTH as user1, regardless of what they do with the auth at NOMAD?

You might know that pynxtools-em is supporting out of the box already a majority of tiff and several proprietary hdf5 based cases from all the big tech em partners so that these developments can be used

representing contrast in two different greyscale images exactly requires the same gamma curve

title: Metainfo Browser for Schema Plugins
text: @FAIRmat-internal-areaD Is there any existing plans to have something like the Metainfo Browser that can be launched directly from a plugin? @Nathan_Daelman  and I were discussing this morning, and think this would fullfill a long-time desire to be able to browse domain-specific Metainfo structures.

title: Metainfo Browser for Schema Plugins
text: The normal metainfo browser should show plugins as a "source" when installed.

title: Metainfo Browser for Schema Plugins
text:
But I guess you want something, where you don't have a nomad installation and just get a browser, e.g. `nomad admin run metainfo-browser`?
yeah, something like that.

title: Metainfo Browser for Schema Plugins
text:
The main case we had in mind is that a user wants to understand the schema structure that is included within a plugin, e.g., from the standard simulation schema used in the central NOMAD (this is linked to how we should add the new schemas that we are developing to the corresponding plugin docs, which is why the question was initially raised). But this then generalizes to a tool where anyone can visualize the schema that they are building, which is nice. In the past, we were always talking about how to point users to the sections/quantities of the metainfo that are actually relevant to their domain. With the move to schema plugins this is now straightforward with such a tool
For install plugins you can use the normal metainfo browser, e.g. here is the `nomad_measurments` on the example oasis: <https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis/gui/analyze/metainfo/nomad_measurements>. Maybe we can improve the metainfo browser, but there is no plan for an external tool/service.
ok thanks a lot, I will have a look
I guess eventually we should hightlight the fact that this comes from plugins better. I think we should at least provide a method for printing out all the (inherited) quantities and subsection of a section.

title: Metainfo Browser for Schema Plugins
text: I raised this in a comment somewhere earlier.

title: Metainfo Browser for Schema Plugins
text: Currently there is a pretty_print method but when we use multiple inheritance it quickly becomes of limited use.

title: Metainfo Browser for Schema Plugins
text:
Technically, one would need to create a smaller version of the UI with a separate build that can then be loaded from a small dev server run from the command line. Not impossible to do, but also quit some work.
@Hampus_Näsström idea could be archives with a CLI command that used m_to_dict parameters to also include the derived   all_* quantities.

text:
Basically when a user wants to extend another plugin, that is not yet included in an Oasis they have access to, it would be good to have (nice) way to see what it already defined in these classes. I'm struggling with this my self from time to time 😅 Another use case is when writing a reader/parser where you would ideally have a template dict that you could just fill. Use cases aren't limited to just for code development, but also data analysis, given that we allow for conversion to `EntryArchive`
A data scientist would arguably not want to devle into the code to understand the data structure and tools at their disposal

title: Migrating to new plugin mechanism - Problem with Parser
text:
Could you give it a try and report back here? https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/plugins/schema_packages.html#schema-package-aliases

@ahmed_ilyas, FYI
Hi @Aaron_Schüller-Ruhl 

There is indeed `aliases` tag for the parsers and most likely that's why it is failing.

title: Migrating to new plugin mechanism - Problem with Parser
text:
Here I am attaching an example configuration for a parser:
```python
from nomad.config.models.plugins import ParserEntryPoint


class TestEntryPoint(ParserEntryPoint):
    def load(self):
        from path_to_test_parser import TestParser

        return TestParser(**self.dict())

test_parser_entry_point = TestEntryPoint(
    name='parsers/test',
    aliases=['parsers/test', 'parsers/test_parser'],
    code_name='test',
    code_homepage='https://www.test.net/',
    description='test pasrer',
    mainfile_mime_re=r'test',
    mainfile_name_re=r'test.json',
)
```

You need to use the `aliases` and set it to your old parser name. this should fix it
Perfect, that worked for me.

title: Migrating to new plugin mechanism - Problem with Parser
text: Thanks. 😀

if you mean task-task connection then the output of the previous task should appear in inputs of the next task, if you refer to in(out)put of workflow-task connection then in(out)put should appear in in(out)puts of the task
So it's not enough to reference another task, it needs to be cross-linked?

i.e.,

```yaml
'workflow2':
...
  'tasks':
  - 'm_def': 'nomad.datamodel.metainfo.workflow.TaskReference'
    'name': 'Task 1'
    'task': '../...workflow2'
    'inputs': []
    'outputs': []
  - 'm_def': 'nomad.datamodel.metainfo.workflow.TaskReference'
    'name': 'Task 2'
    'task': '../...workflow2'
    'inputs':
    - 'name': 'input from Task 1'
      'section': '../<path to Task 1 mainfile>#/run/None/system/-1'
    ...
```

would not make a connection but

```yaml
'workflow2':
...
  'tasks':
  - 'm_def': 'nomad.datamodel.metainfo.workflow.TaskReference'
    'name': 'Task 1'
    'task': '../...workflow2'
    'inputs': []
    'outputs': 
    - 'name': 'output system from Task 1'
      'section': '../<path to Task 1 mainfile>#/run/None/system/-1'
  - 'm_def': 'nomad.datamodel.metainfo.workflow.TaskReference'
    'name': 'Task 2'
    'task': '../...workflow2'
    'inputs':
    - 'name': 'output system from Task 1'
      'section': '../<path to Task 1 mainfile>#/run/None/system/-1'
    ...
```
would make the connection?

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: I was presenting FAIR data, FAIRmat and NOMAD yesterday, and I got a lot of questions about how to modify/remove data that has already been uploaded.

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: Or the possibility to add more comments, or additional analysis to an entry.

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text:
Because I'm mostly focused on formatting the data at the moment, I don't have a lot of experience with data curation. So here are for example a few more practical questions I was asked.

- Imagine you have a setup that directly outputs data to your NOMAD OASIS, can you then modify, delete, annotate the data sets, add some field to it etc.?
- Imagine you have an already published experimental dataset and you discover a calibration error. Can you correct it? And if yes, is there some kind of versioning, so that maybe people having used this dataset before see that what is in now is different from what they have used?
- If you change affiliation, can you update it in your datasets? So yes, the question would be: what is possible? and until which level of sharing. (like how is the answer when the data is unloaded but not shared, how is it once it's shared, and how is it once it has a DOI for example).

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: Hi, I just want to comment on the "data curation" part. Data curation means that there is some control on the quality of the entries that are being generated, but NOMAD is not like that. In the central service we accept any kind of data.

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text:
Now, an Oasis is a different thing: you can decide how to handle this. Now:

> * Imagine you have a setup that directly outputs data to your NOMAD OASIS, can you then modify, delete, annotate the data sets, add some field to it etc.?

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: Being admin of your oasis, you can essentially re-run/re-process entries at will (https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/oasis/admin.html).

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: You could have a script running at a different moment in time to run your entries to modify, delete, annotate fields...

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: This can be done creating and maintaining a parser plugin.

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text:
This is also how the central NOMAD works: we have some parsers that change over time, and we reprocess sometimes some of the entries present in the database (either to fix bugs, to populate new fields, or change some).

> * Imagine you have an already published experimental dataset and you discover a calibration error.

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: Can you correct it?

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: And if yes, is there some kind of versioning, so that maybe people having used this dataset before see that what is in now is different from what they have used?

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: My previous comment answers the first part of the question. Sadly, for the versioning part, no, we don't control the version of an entry in the database. So once you reprocess an entry, the information is overwritten and we don't keep any registry of the previous entries.

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: I think this is a feature request.

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text:
Maybe @Lauri_Himanen can tell you more, but for sure, we didn't start working on it.

> * If you change affiliation, can you update it in your datasets? There is another issue regarding affiliation, but I don't know whether it is updated in the datasets. Good question, maybe @Sascha_Klawohn knows 🙂
Hi @Julien@KAUST,

Currently, we only have fairly primitive ways to update data:
 - If your data is not published, you can change it at any time, but any old data will be overwritten
- If your data is published, the **raw data** becomes immutable and you cannot change it. If you want to correct something, you will need to make a new upload/dataset with the corrected data.

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: This process is similar to scientific articles: errata can be added, but the original publication cannot be changed.

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: Note that any **derived data** e.g. data produced by a parser can change, and often does since we improve the parsers fairly regularly.

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: Author affiliation can be changed, since it is not tied to an upload/dataset.

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
text: We are currently having some problems with the form that allows you to do it, this is discussed here: https://discord.com/channels/[REDACTED_PHONE]/[REDACTED_PHONE]/threads/[REDACTED_PHONE]

I understand what you mean! I just thought having all the simulations that belong to the calculation of a single property in one upload is much easier for the upload and naming process, even if I can use workflows to connect separate uploads. Especially the limit of 10 unpublished uploads makes splitting uploads a little bit inconvenient, because I would prefer to first completely upload a connected set of entries, check if everything is fine, and then publish it. How would you suggest proceeding with the current uploads? I need them for a paper submission, and I am basically just waiting for the DOI of the data. Therefore, it would be great if I could get the entries published today. Since all the uploads are still stuck at processing, I can't delete them or upload anything new. Yeah I totally understand that it is more inconvenient. In principal the way to go would be to write a script to send smaller uploads only with "regular" entries (ie, no workflows), 10 at a time, check for successful processing, then publish and move on to the next ten. This at least is how I have approached it with a postdoc I am working with. I could send a script later. During this process I would store the entry and upload IDs in whatever organization you need to later make the workflows or datasets, these you can perform after the entries are published.

title: NOMAD Oasis v1.3.16 - Failed docker image build due to removed package 'distutils' in Python3.12
text: Hi all, I try to build a docker image, which I did previous successfully with Oasis v1.3.14. Now I'm trying to bump a new version [Oasis v1.3.16](https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/tree/v1.3.16?ref_type=tags) (commit: 80d588ab335c5da9) as basis for a plugin and some custom webpages in the UI(, which both are not part in the following). First I tried, the unmodified v1.3.16 out of the box via the gitlab repo and setup the development environment (with local keycloak) -> everything is working and no problems. But if I create the docker image via the attached Dockerfile the image is build (after correcting some minor errors in the file: see below). But if I launch the image the 'nomad_oasis_app' does not start as the error log states, that some plugin-modules use the deprecated/remove 'distutils' package in python3.12 - see the attached log.

title: NOMAD Oasis v1.3.16 - Failed docker image build due to removed package 'distutils' in Python3.12
text: Can you please check or give me find, which module/plugin causes the problem?

title: NOMAD Oasis v1.3.16 - Failed docker image build due to removed package 'distutils' in Python3.12
text: I looks like 'phonopy_parser_entry_point' with the old version phonopy==2.11.0 [release [REDACTED_PHONE]](https://pypi.org/project/phonopy/#history) in [requirements-plugins.txt](https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/v1.3.16/requirements-plugins.txt?ref_type=tags#L87)  Thanks.

title: NOMAD Oasis v1.3.16 - Failed docker image build due to removed package 'distutils' in Python3.12
text: Btw: the official python version for NOMAD is Python3.12, nor?

title: NOMAD Oasis v1.3.16 - Failed docker image build due to removed package 'distutils' in Python3.12
text:
Also: in the Dockerfile the folder 'example' is missing in 'dev_node'. Maybe you add it in Line 130-132:
```
COPY nomad ./nomad
COPY examples ./examples
COPY scripts ./scripts
```
also you changed the name of the 'nomad-lab-\*.tar.gz' to 'nomad_lab-\*.tar.gz' in builder. You may change also the lines L168-170:

> `# install
> # note the underscore '_' instead of '-' in nomad_lab in v1.3.16
> COPY --from=dev_package /app/dist/nomad_lab-*.tar.gz .
> RUN pip install nomad_lab-*.tar.gz
> ````
Building the image for deployment via `nomad-FAIR` repo is no longer supported, please use https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-distro-template to build deployment docker images
👍  thanks for the advice. Please remove then the 'docker image building part' from the [documentation](https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/develop/setup.html#build-the-docker-image). #closed
thanks! we will update that
I tested the [nomad-distro-template](https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-distro-template), but it seems to download/install/set-up the oasis version 1.3.15? As I looked in the docker-compose and referring images: the [main](https://github.com/fairmat-nfdi/nomad-distro-template/pkgs/container/nomad-distro-template) does not contain a specific version number. How do I know with NOMAD Oasis version is in the "main-package"? You can specify it as a version constraint in dependencies: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-distro-template/blob/main/pyproject.toml#L23
`nomad-lab[parsing, infrastructure]>=1.3.16` or 
`nomad-lab[parsing, infrastructure]==1.3.16`

title: Nexus Search very slow for larger entry numbers
text:
I have noticed that the searching for nexus quantities is really slow, once there are a sizable number or results (100-200), and a number of search widgets are on the dashboard. https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/jumpToReference/nomad/search.py#L510
In particular,  this line seems to be relevant:
https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/jumpToReference/nomad/search.py#L525

title: NotImplementedError for context.raw_path_exists method
text:
When running nomad installation with app_worker, the `m_context` is an instance of the class `nomad.datamodel.context.ServerContext` which has a well-defined `raw_path_exists` method. But while running pytests, which uses the nomad as a package (and not the local installation), `m_context` is an instance of class `nomad.datamodel.context.Context`  in which `raw_path_exists` is not implemented. Because of this difference, the pytests fail during the plugin testing, when using `archive.m_context.raw_path_exists(file_path)`. Can we harmonize these two methods for the classes `ServerContext` and `Context`? Clarification: when using pytest, `m_context` is of class `nomad.datamodel.context.ClientContext` which inherits `nomad.datamode.context.Context`, and as the child doesn't have its own `raw_path_exists` method, it defaults to the one from the parent, which is not implemented. Something like this could resolve it
https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/commit/2e8d24cd139cef1a9b9b7244c25c00b4333f28f1
I guess we only implemented the Context interface based on what was necessary. The ServerContext is the more complete one as this is used all the time in the app. The ClientContext is only used by, well, clients. E.g. when you do the "nomad parse" command, or the ArchiveQuery instantiates metainfo object froms the retrieved data. For the first case "nomad parse" you commit seems ok. For the second one, it does not hurt, but it also does not help, as the raw data is most likely not locally available. I guess it complements the existing `raw...` functions.

title: NotImplementedError for context.raw_path_exists method
text: Feel free to create an MR and merge this.

title: NotImplementedError for context.raw_path_exists method
text: I agree!

title: NotImplementedError for context.raw_path_exists method
text: At least this resolves an error I face during a pytest for plugin that uses m_context.raw_path_exists method. I will go ahead and make a MR for this.

title: NotImplementedError for context.raw_path_exists method
text: Thanks!
