title: Oasis Docker image CI not fetching the right parser repository in unit tests
text:
I have a question concerning the unit tests in the CI of the `nomad-distro-template`. I create a repository (https://github.com/AddMorePower/oasis-docker-image) based on this template to create the Docker image for my Oasis where I plugged 4 of my plugins
In the pyproject.toml of my repo
```
plugins = [
  "nomad-damask-parser @ git+https://github.com/AddMorePower/nomad-DAMASK_parser.git@v0.1.0",
  "nomad-sxdm-parser @ git+https://github.com/AddMorePower/nomad-SXDM_parser.git@v0.1.0",
  "nomad-txrm-parser @ git+https://github.com/AddMorePower/nomad-TXRM_parser.git@v0.1.0",
  "nomad-ebsd-parser @ git+https://github.com/AddMorePower/nomad-EBSD_parser.git@v0.1.0",
```
However, in the CI to build and publish the Docker image, the unit tests concerning these plugins are failing because for some reason, the url repository is not the same as in the `.toml`, for instance, the tests are trying to download the plugin code in https://github.com/Guillaume-Gaisne/nomad_ebsd_parser, instead of https://github.com/AddMorePower/nomad-EBSD_parser as specified in the .toml file. I have to precise that when I removed all occurrences of `Guillaume-Gaisne` in both the repository of my plugins and the repository of the Docker image creation. A second point is that when I instantiated the parser plugin repository with the cookiecutter option, I inputed "Guillaume-Gaisne" in the `github_username` question (but I turned this to "AddMorePower in the `.cruft.json` file of each of my repo. The Docker image is well created and the plugins are correctly loaded but this small point in the unit tests is bugging me as I can't find the reason why it is looking for a different repo than the ones specified in the `.toml` file.

title: Oasis Docker image CI not fetching the right parser repository in unit tests
text:
Here is the log for the unit tests of the EBSD parser:
```
[REDACTED_PHONE]:34:47,082 - nomad_ebsd_parser - INFO - Starting test for package: PluginPackage(name='nomad_ebsd_parser', package_name='nomad_ebsd_parser', description='NOMAD plugin for EBSD measurements within AddMorePower', version='0.1.0', homepage=None, documentation=None, repository='https://github.com/Guillaume-Gaisne/nomad_ebsd_parser', github_url='https://github.com/Guillaume-Gaisne/nomad_ebsd_parser', commit_hash=None, entry_points=['nomad_ebsd_parser.parsers:parser_entry_point'])
[REDACTED_PHONE]:34:47,209 - nomad_ebsd_parser - ERROR - Command 'git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Guillaume-Gaisne/nomad_ebsd_parser /tmp/tmp6lqfn258' failed. Return code: 128, stdout: , stderr: Cloning into '/tmp/tmp6lqfn258'...
fatal: could not read Username for 'https://github.com': No such device or address

[REDACTED_PHONE]:34:47,210 - nomad_ebsd_parser - ERROR - Failed to clone repository for 'nomad_ebsd_parser'.
[REDACTED_PHONE]:34:47,210 - nomad_ebsd_parser - ERROR - Package test failed for 'nomad_ebsd_parser': Failed to clone and checkout 'nomad_ebsd_parser'```
Also, when I created my different plugins, I used `Guillaume-Gaisne` for the Github username in the cookiecutter initialisation session for 3 of the 4 parsers: the DAMASK, EBSD and SXDM ones and the unit tests are wrongly looking for the code in https://github.com/Guillaume-Gaisne, where for the TXRM parser, it seems that I used `AddMorePower` as the Github username in the initialisation procedure and the unit tests are correctly looking for the code in https://github.com/AddMorePower and the unit tests pass only for this package
I also deleted all the caches to avoid the fact that the CI could use the repository url in the caches with `Guillaume-Gaisne` instead of `AddMorePower` between my changes when I turned all occurrences of `Guillaume-Gaisne` in `AddMorePower` but deleting the caches didn't change the behaviour of the unit tests. Bit weird, I'll check Monday and let you know
I have investigated this a bit further, and I think the `nomad-plugin-tests` package is working as intended here.

title: Oasis Docker image CI not fetching the right parser repository in unit tests
text:
Checking the metadata for the ebsd library points to your personal github URL:

```shell
❯ uv run python
Python 3.12.9 (main, Mar [REDACTED_PHONE], 21:36:21) [Clang 20.1.0 ] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
> >>> from importlib.metadata import metadata
> >>> metadata('nomad_ebsd_parser').json['project_url']
['Repository, https://github.com/Guillaume-Gaisne/nomad_ebsd_parser']
```

Which also matches the project metadata from the .venv

```shell
❯ cat .venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/nomad_ebsd_parser-0.1.0.dist-info/METADATA | rg Project-URL
Project-URL: Repository, https://github.com/Guillaume-Gaisne/nomad_ebsd_parser
```

Seems like the ebsd parser version in the lock file is a little older. Running `uv lock --upgrade-package nomad_ebsd_parser` upgrades the ebsd version and fixes the repository URL.

```shell
❯ cat .venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/nomad_ebsd_parser-0.1.0.dist-info/METADATA | rg Project-URL
Project-URL: Repository, https://github.com/Guillaume-Gaisne/nomad_ebsd_parser
```
Thanks a lot !

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: We are currently trying to upload our data backlog to our local Oasis.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: The files are all converted to the NeXus format and correctly parsed. However, after rough 100-200 uploaded files, the server PC freezes and needs to be restarted manually. We noticed that during uploading the swap memory size gradually increases until it is full and the server freezes. This only happens when uploading nexus files and happens also with the example ellipsometry .nxs file (when uploaded multiple times under different names).

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: Has anybody seen something similar or advice on how to handle this?

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text:
Dear Johannes,
Given that you use a local oasis, providing a little more context would be good to enable at all a search for answers to your observations.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text:
These are guiding questions:
* Were all files uploaded into one upload or multiple at once, how (API call, drag-and-drop?)
* Was the total data volume (in GB) in the order of the amount of main memory that was (made available to the container or is available physically left on the server), your message suggests you hit main memory limits and end up swapping, combined with a server with a spinning disk and yay...
* Which version of Oasis and plugins are you using?
* The ellipsometry example as it is shipped is tiny (provided it was not modified with own data) there might be parsing errors, in which case the logs record these issues but that should not make the system completely unresponsive.
* Similar, uploads might get stuck sometimes but still the operation system remains operational, so in the case of the ellipsometry example, I guess you made an empty upload and dragged and dropped the NeXus file.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: Did this nevertheless then sent the server into frozen state?

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text:
Dear Markus, thank you for your response, i will try to provide a more detailed account of our setup and tests:
- I work with API calls made with a python script and the request package. I tried both creating an upload per file as well as using a PUT request to add files to an existing upload, both with the same result. I always make sure a file is fully processed before uploading the next file, either via checking the upload status (in a 3s loop, as suggested in the docs) or the 'wait_for_processing' parameter in the PUT request. 
- The .nxs files in question are converted from one of our laboratory instruments and all have 1.3MB. Even 200 such are much smaller than the 8GB RAM and 4GB swap memory we work with on the server. We do hit the main memory limit before utilizing the swap memory, but it never resets and eventually no swap memory is left and the freeze happens. At this point, we were not able to do anything but manually restart the computer via the power button, it does not respond to anything.
- We are running Oasis version 1.3.13 and pynxtools version 0.9.3
- I did all my testing via python script and requests. I can test drag-and-drop tomorrow and get back to you. So far I used drag and drop only to check whether the parser is running successful or not. Our goal is to build an automated data pipline from the instrument(s) to our Oasis.
- After a freeze an upload is usually stuck in the processing state, which i then reset via the admin console. The server is responsive in this state.
- I tried to use the ellipsometry example to exclude problems with our NeXus files, although the parser always matches and runs successfully for our files.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: I now see that there are a bunch warnings (40+) in the logs after the parser has run both for our nexus files as well as for the ellipsometry file. 'WARNING: pynxtools.nomad.entrypoints:nexus_parser | error while setting field' (or attribute).

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: However, no errors.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text:
Sounds very like some memory leak problem in the handling of hdf5 files.
@Johannes_Dallmann thank you very much for your detailed report of your observations, these mirror also some of our experiences where we faced issues with the memory consumption of the current implementation for the NeXus/HDF5 parsing and normalization pipeline.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: I created an issue on our board for this to not forget about it.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: Mind thought that the root cause can range from sth trivial to fix to something which may demand time consuming memory profiling a non trivial multi-threaded application.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: Which CPU is this server powered by?

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: It is good to know that you observe this relevant high mem consumption even when you upload via API calls only one file at a time, i.e. sequential uploads.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: Mind though that 8GB in total is not much, specifically if 4GB is reserved here for swap, that seems a quite fraction offered for swap.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: Nevertheless, your expectation is right, ideally processing an a few MB file should NOT demand allocating dozens of GBs.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: I assume that you run this OASIS then essentially more like local development installation, right?

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: Meaning there is no container orchestrator (e.g. k8s) that would kick the container in which the NOMAD worker runs from the system if the container takes too much memory like what is the case on the production NOMAD that runs on servers. Cuz typically the container scheduler should protect the OS from having one container attempting to drain that much - all - 🙂 memory. It is rather this last bit which in my opinion makes the issue more severe than just an issue like well NOMAD may demand much main memory, the system should never ideally become that frozen.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: Hi @Markus_Kuehbach, I'm running the development Nomad Oasis together with Johannes.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: Thanks for your really fast help so far.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text:
Our CPU on the development system is:
   - AMD Phenom(tm) II X6 1055T Processor
   - 64-bit, 6 cores, 3GHz freq. per core

Initially we had 1GB of swap accompanying our RAM.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: We increased just to see if that would allow the uploads to be stable for longer, which didn't really work.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: I was looking into ways to limit docker memory allocation size, but am still relatively new to it.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text: Our docker images have a max.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text:
Memory allocation of 2GiB according to the docker service. Is there an easy way to keep the docker images from bleeding the memory leak? I will have a look towards the orchastrators in the meantime.
first this is the a reference for such questions https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#understand-the-risks-of-running-out-of-memory
https://jasebell.medium.com/a-little-bit-more-on-docker-memory-management-3a3c3ff95ce0#:~:text=Memory%20Limits%20in%20Docker&text=This%20will%20start%20a%20container,limit%2C%20it%20will%20be%20terminated.
@Johannes_Dallmann The latest master branch of pynxtools contains a fix for this.

title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
text:
Once we have a new release, I will update you here
That's great, thank you

title: Overwriting subsections with quantities not possible?
text: Is it intended that MetaInfo does not allow to overwrite a SubSection with a Quantity?

title: Overwriting subsections with quantities not possible?
text:
For example: 
```py
class MyProcess(Process): 
  steps = Quantity(
    type = str, 
    description = 'The steps of the process.',
    a_eln = ELNAnnotation(component=ELNComponentEnum.StringEditQuantity),
    repeats=True,
  )```
raises an: 
`nomad.metainfo.metainfo.MetainfoError: Cannot inherit from different property types.`
since in the basesections, `Process` has already a repeating SubSection called that way. 
```py
class Process(Activity):
  ...
  steps = SubSection(
    section_def=ProcessStep,
    description="""
    An ordered list of all the dependant steps that make up this activity.
    """,
    repeats=True,
  )
  ...
```
~~I have not tested it the other way around yet.~~
The other way around as well.

title: Overwriting subsections with quantities not possible?
text: Yes, that is very much intended!

title: Parsing Polarizabilities and Dipole Moments
text: Hi, I have simulations run with the code FHI-AIMS.

title: Parsing Polarizabilities and Dipole Moments
text: They are Molecular Dynamics and Single Point calculations.

title: Parsing Polarizabilities and Dipole Moments
text: The Molecular Dynamics calculations include the Total Dipole Moment, which is not parsed in the data processing. The Single Point calculations calculate polarizabilities. But when uploaded the polarizability tensor is also not parsed. I would like to ask the developers to include  the parsing of the dipole moment and the polarizabilites? They are expensive calculations and necessary for the calculation of anharmonic IR and Raman spectra.

Let me know if you need further information or any help I can provide,

title: Pint and pydantic update
text: Hi @Carla_Terboven!

title: Pint and pydantic update
text:
We do have issues for both of these libraries: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/2142 and https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/2168

The main problem is that both updates require quite a bit of work, as in the case of Pydantic, we need to update our code to use the new interface, and in the case of Pint, we would need to change several of our parsers due to this issue: https://github.com/hgrecco/pint/issues/1809

So I cannot promise that we will do this migration very urgently, as there are not yet that many plugins that would require this migration.

title: Pint and pydantic update
text:
Ok I see, thank you very much for the update @Lauri_Himanen . I would be very happy if you could find the time for the issue at some point. 🫶
@Carla_Terboven: Update here: we have now updated to Pydantic 2.0.0 in nomad v1.3.15, and we will try to get the update for Pint into v1.3.16 which will should be released late-April. Wow, thank you very much for the effort!

title: PlotSection for Static Image
text:
Hi NOMAD Team,
I asked myself if there's any built-in functionality to plot/show an image such as `.tif` as these are the results of [SEM measurements](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scanning_electron_microscope)?

title: PlotSection for Static Image
text:
Therefore I came up to "abuse" plotly to create a plot section and display the image as [static background](https://plotly.com/python/images/#zoom-on-static-images):

``` python
class MeasurementSEM(ELNMeasurement, PlotSection, ArchiveSection):
    '''
    Class for handling measurement of SEM.
    '''
    m_def = Section(
        label='Measurement-SEM',
        a_eln={"overview": True,},
    )
    
    data_file = Quantity(
        type=str,
        description='''
        A reference to an uploaded .tif produced by the SEM instrument.
        ''',
        a_browser={"adaptor": "RawFileAdaptor"},
        a_eln={"component": "FileEditQuantity"},
    )

    def normalize(self, archive: 'EntryArchive', logger: 'BoundLogger'):
        """
        The normalize function of the `MeasurementSEM` section.
        """
        
        try:
            
            # Check if any file is provided
            if self.data_file:
                # Check if the file has the correct extension
                if not self.data_file.endswith('.tif'):
                    if not self.data_file.endswith('.tiff'):
                        raise DataFileError(f"The file '{self.data_file}' must have a .tif or .tiff extension.")
            
```
``` python
# continued normalized fct
                # Otherwise parse the file as binary
                with archive.m_context.raw_file(self.data_file, 'rb') as imagefile:
                    with Image.open(imagefile) as img:
                        # Get the size of the image
                        img_width, img_height = img.size
                         # Convert the image to RGB (necessary for JPEG)
                        img = img.convert('RGB')
                        # Create a BytesIO object to hold the image data
                        buffered = io.BytesIO()
                        # Save the image to the BytesIO object in JPEG format
                        img.save(buffered, format="JPEG")
                        # Get the byte data
                        img_str = base64.b64encode(buffered.getvalue()).decode('utf-8')
                        # Create the URI image string
                        uri = f"data:image/jpeg;base64,{img_str}"  
```
``` python
                        # see https://plotly.com/python/images/#zoom-on-static-images
                        fig = go.Figure()
                        scale_factor = 1.0
                        fig.add_trace(
                            go.Scatter(
                                x=[0, img_width * scale_factor],
                                y=[0, img_height * scale_factor],
                                mode="markers",
                                marker_opacity=0
                            )
                        )
                        # Configure axes
                        fig.update_xaxes(
                            visible=False,
                            range=[0, img_width * scale_factor]
                        )

                        fig.update_yaxes(
                            visible=False,
                            range=[0, img_height * scale_factor],
                            # the scaleanchor attribute ensures that the aspect ratio stays constant
                            scaleanchor="x"
                        )
                        
                        # Add image
                        fig.add_layout_image(
                            dict(
                                x=0,
                                sizex=img_width * scale_factor,
                                y=img_height * scale_factor,
                                sizey=img_height * scale_factor,
                                xref="x",
                                yref="y",
                                opacity=1.0,
                                layer="below",
                                sizing="stretch",
                                source=uri)
                        ) 
```
``` python
                        # Configure other layout
                        fig.update_layout(
                            width=img_width * scale_factor,
                            height=img_height * scale_factor,
                            margin={"l": 0, "r": 0, "t": 0, "b": 0},
                        )
                        
                        # convert to JSON and add to archive
                        figure_json = fig.to_plotly_json()
                        figure_json['config'] = {'staticPlot': True}
                        self.figures = [PlotlyFigure(label=f'Measurement SEM: {self.data_file}', index=0, figure=figure_json)]
            
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error('Invalid file extension for parsing.', exc_info=e)
        
        super().normalize(archive, logger)

```
I share the code just for curiosity how to transfer a `.tif` to `.jpeg` to `Base64` image (, but still lacking any zoom functionality, albeit the preview is working).

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text: Hi NOMAD-Team, thanks for the great tutorial 13 on plugins https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=c99XEC6GPfE and how to create them.

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text: During plugin development I stumbled about a question, and maybe somebody knows a solution for that: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity?

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text: As use-case: say you have an ELNSample which holds maybe a list of other ELNSamples is was derived from- something like data linage/sample history.

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text:
I used the following (exemplary code):
```
class CRCSample(ElnWithFormulaBaseSection, ReadableIdentifiers, EntryData, ArchiveSection):

    m_def = Section()
    
    Sample_reference_from_collaboration = Quantity(
        type=CRCSample,
        description='If sample is received by collaboration, then reference it here.',
        a_eln={
            "component": "ReferenceEditQuantity"
        },
        shape=["*"],
    )
    
    ### ... and more
    ### but the line with 'type=CRCSample,' throws a passing error
    ### if I reference another class, everything parses
```
If they are defined in the same python file you can give the type as a string
However I would not recommend using the `ElnWithFormulaBaseSection` as it is being depricated
But this is how you would do it:
```py
class CRCSample(CompositeSystem, EntryData):

    m_def = Section()
    
    sample_reference_from_collaboration = Quantity(
        type='CRCSample',
        description='If sample is received by collaboration, then reference it here.',
        a_eln={
            "component": "ReferenceEditQuantity"
        },
        shape=["*"],
    )
```
🤩  Thanks for the quick reply! Indeed, changing the line from `type=CRCSample` with apostrophe to `type='CRCSample'` solved the problem.
😮 Thanks also for the hint about the deprecated `ElnWithFormulaBaseSection`.

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text: I didn't see in NOMAD Metainfo Browser any `deprecated` flag, but I'll change that.

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text:
Any advice for any alternative for the very convenient function/quantity `chemical_formula` as `CompositeSystem`  does not do the same thing? 🙇‍♂️
It's a bit hard for us to put in the deprecation warning but I think we need to improve there.

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text: It's listed under legacy sections in the python file but I fully understand that most people won't check that.

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text:
You can have a look at the PureSubstance and PureSubstanceSection classes
They should provide a lookup and normalization of a chemical formula
A sample is very rarely a pure single material though so we typically recommend using `CompositeSystem` to describe samples and then use the `PureSubstanceSection` in the components list to list any material that makes up it. You can see an example of this in the https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/gui/analyze/metainfo/nomad/[REDACTED_EMAIL] which is a `CompositeSystem`
It does becom a bit nested though:https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/gui/analyze/metainfo/nomad/[REDACTED_EMAIL]/components/[REDACTED_EMAIL]/pure_substance/molecular_formula
So if you want users to fill this field manually you could place a custom eln quantity on the top level of your schema and fill this nested quantity during the normalization. We are working on simplifying this over nesting in version 2 of the base sections but unfortunately we have not yet finished the general normalization for those. Thank you for the detailed answer. Indeed the `ELNSubstance` or `ELNSample` has the feature of elemental_composition.

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text: I will also have a detailed look that again as I try them out before.

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text: Just for clarification of the use case: `chemical_formula` breaks down any (almost) arbitrary (crystal no chemical exact) elemental composition e.g.

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text:
Ra0.2F0.6B0.3 (made up example) in one rush and indexes the elements for the 'Explore/Periodic table' as really neat feature search capabilities for non-chemist-collaboration partners.
 ...after some digging...

title: Plugin-Development: How to self-reference a class with ReferenceEditQuantity
text:
Yes, the `ELNSample->pure substance->PureSubstanceSection->molecular_formula` does the exact same behavior.
https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/gui/analyze/metainfo/nomad/[REDACTED_EMAIL]/pure_substance/molecular_formula
Thanks again!

title: Preview HDF5 only works when logged in
text:

title: Problem building Oasis image
text:
Yesterday, I decided to upgrade my repository that create the Docker image for my Oasis. I followed the changes and included my plugins in the `.toml` file and it works perfectly on my personal Github account. However, in the AddMorePower project, we created a github account to centralise all the developments in the project and I am now using this project repository to generate the Oasis image. With the previous way of doing it (with the `plugins.txt` file), it was working correctly. But I decided to switch to the new system, as it was working correctly on my personal account but this time, I have some troubles when I run the workflow `Build and publish docker images`... In this workflow, the `Update Python Lock file` and `Build Docker Image (jupyter)` are passing but the `Build Docker Image (app)` is failing with this error:

#27 exporting to image

title: Problem with SectionProxy
text:
Hey, I wanted to use a SectionProxy, becasue I reference a Section before defining it in the same python script. In the section`UMR_InternalSubstrate `I have the following quantity:

`standard_substrate_lot = Quantity(`
`        type=SectionProxy('UMR_StandardSubstrateLot'),`
`        a_eln=dict(component='ReferenceEditQuantity'))`

And in the same script below I define the Section:
`class UMR_StandardSubstrateLot(EntryData): `
`    m_def = Section()`

But the App Container is unhealthy and I get the following error:
`nomad.metainfo.metainfo.MetainfoError: Type UMR_schemas.substrate.UMR_StandardSubstrateLot:Section of nomad.metainfo.metainfo.Quantity.type:Quantity is not a valid metainfo quantity type`

Any ideas what I am missing out here? This are the full logs of the App Container:
Define UMR_StandardSubstrateLot first. But that's what I exactly don't want to /can not do and why I want to use the SectionProxy. Otherwise I could use a normal Reference.🤔
If I understood it correctly the SectionProxy is used if you define a section with the section class itself. For example if you have a section Process Step which can have Substeps with the same attributes you can do:
```python
class ProcessStep(ArchiveSection):
    m_def = Section(label_quantity='name')

    name = Quantity(
        type=str,
        a_eln=dict(
            component='StringEditQuantity'))

    steps = SubSection(
        section_def=SectionProxy('ProcessStep'), repeats=True)
```

title: Problems with data types after updating to NOMAD develop branch
text: Hi, I  get errors when processing data from raw files in a plugin after switching to develop branch (1.3.3.dev142+g56585ed05). Before on NOMAD v1.3.3 the data was processed without errors.

title: Problems with data types after updating to NOMAD develop branch
text:
NOMAD develop seems to be less "tolerant" with incoming data:
e.g. does not convert "20" into a float:
```
raise ValueError(f'Cannot convert {value} to {self._dtype}.') ValueError: Cannot convert 20 to <class 'numpy.float64'>."
```
I am now adjusting my file reader to explicitly convert the data to e.g. float for example above. But still I was wondering if NOMAD should not convert `'20'` into a float `20.0` as it did before. @Markus_Scheidgen
Maybe a guess, but could be worth a try, add a dot cuz the string "20" otherwise is unclear and maps more to an integer than a float, "20." is a different statement
This is due to the new metainfo datatypes merged recently.

title: Problems with data types after updating to NOMAD develop branch
text: And yes they are less forgiving now.

title: Problems with data types after updating to NOMAD develop branch
text: Auto converting strings into numbers is really a stretch.

title: Problems with data types after updating to NOMAD develop branch
text: This conversion from string to float is really the parsers job. @Theodore_Chang_DM_BITTE whats your opinion on this?

title: Problems with data types after updating to NOMAD develop branch
text: The type system internally tries to convert the input data into the target type. In this particular case, when a string "20" is given, the type instance tries to check the following: "20" == str(numpy.float64("20")), this results actual check to be something like "20" == "[REDACTED_PHONE]" due to the inexact nature of floats. The new system tries to be as smart as possible but for cases like this, the risk of loss of precision cannot be avoided. I'll say the easy fix is assign the correct value to the quantity, meaning that instead of "20", use numpy.float64("20").

title: Problems with data types after updating to NOMAD develop branch
text: Thanks for clarifying.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text:
Hi NOMAD-Team,
maybe this feature request is a little bit orthogonal to the idea of NOMAD, Oasis, and data sharing 🫣  By launching an Oasis to a dedicated user community, there's maybe the need to protect the "Search/Entry" navigation and also the API against unauthorized access. This can be the case, if one wants "easy sharing" of research data only within a community or facility without exploiting all "public uploads" to everyone, who knows the IP of the Oasis. AFAIK, following files may be updated then (hard coded in the oasis):
gui/src/components/search/SearchPage.js
```
+ import { withLoginRequired } from '../api'
- export default SearchPage
+ export default withLoginRequired(SearchPage, 'Please login to search all public available data.')
``` 
nomad/search.py
```
# Restrict all "/entries" API calls to authentication method with login credentials
# change the line in def _owner_es_query() in lines 608++
...
if owner == 'all':
        if user_id is None:
            raise AuthenticationRequiredError(
                'Authentication required for owner value user.'
            )
        q = query(published=True)
        q |= viewers_query(user_id, force_groups=True)
    elif owner == 'public':
        if user_id is None:
            raise AuthenticationRequiredError(
                'Authentication required for owner value user.'
            )
        q = query(published=True) & query(with_embargo=False)
    elif owner == 'visible':
        if user_id is None:
            raise AuthenticationRequiredError(
                'Authentication required for owner value user.'
            )
        q = query(published=True) & query(with_embargo=False)
        q |= viewers_query(user_id, force_groups=True)
    elif owner == 'shared':
```
Maybe you can create a "flag/option" to the configs/nomad.yaml like:
```
oasis:
  is_protected: true
```
to restrict the access on demand when deploying an oasis, if one need this extended authorization protection.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: What do you think? Hi @ron_do!

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: So far we have not thought about this kind of usage.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: Some people do limit access to the Oasis by only hosting it in an internal network which can be connected to only through e.g. a VPN + credentials.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: That seems like a very systematic way of limiting access.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text:
In principle what you suggest is doable, but we would need to investigate quite a lot of time to properly check and ensure that this flag correctly protects all of the endpoints etc. So I would say that at this point we are not willing to implement this, unless we hear from multiple sources that this would be a necessary feature. I would encourage trying out other solutions for now: restrict the access to the machine by requiring the use of a VPN service OR then do not make any of the data publicly available, but only share it with a limited set of users. Is not the option oasis:allowed_users: doing this? With the setting, I cannot access the oasis at all, unless a user in this list is logged in
Maybe I don't understand the question completely
If I understood correctly, the question was more about controlling access without a special whitelist that would need to be maintained. But you are @Laurenz_Rettig definitely right about `oasis.allowed_users`: with this list you can control accesss with a pretty fine granularity: I did not realize we even had this 😅  

Maybe @ron_do could comment if `allowed_users` would be an option for you? Implementing something like the proposed `is_protected` would not actually be that hard if we simply follow a similar strategy to `allowed_users` in the codebase. Hi Lauri, Hi Laurenz,
thank you for your reply and discussing my request 🙂 Maybe I should explain in more detail, that I am aiming for: I'm setting up an Oasis in a protected "University network" as one common data sharing platform for several groups (-> hence a Collaborative Research Centre).

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text:
Therefore externals can only access the oasis with usage of an VPN tunnel ✅  
Problem statement/use case: But also within the "University network" the "public part" of the oasis - the gui/search interface (e.g. https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis/gui/search/eln ) and also the public API can be accessed by all people who know the IP address of the dedicated oasis (and maybe they are not part of any associated working groups). As there is a very nice convenient feature for "Uploads in Step3" with the checkbox: "Edit visibility and access; Enabling this will allow all users, including guests without an account, to view the upload even before it is published." to share unpublished quite recent research data between the working groups for review not bothering you to select a special group or person. Also "enabling visibility of unpublished data" allows for data changes e.g. some data have been updated in contrast to "published data in the oasis can not be altered (step 5)". So, I am searching for a workflow to share all ("not yet publishable") data to everybody, who has access to the oasis, but not exposing recent research data to the world/university. I also thought about using `allowed_users` in the yaml file, but there are more than 100 people to take care off and this can be cumbersome. Also this does not solve the problem to restrict the "search" ability only to known users as the data will be still exposed.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: Does it make sense to you?

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text:
How do you want to define the list of users that are "allowed"? You somewhere have to provide this list. Doing it with allowed_users, you have to restart the oasis when you want to change this list, I believe
And I do believe it also restricts the API to access these files, but I am not 100% sure of this
What would make sense is the option to use your own key_cloak, and restrict access to only logged in users. Is that what you want? I know the name of the scientists working for the Collaborative Research Centre - that not a problem at all. I also use a local keycloak instance to keep track of them and to provide the access (and to delete if necessary). In the future I will also bind the keycloak with our university "identity management system" to use a more official login/access control. (If students or PI leave they will automatically loose the account and cannot access the oasis - and vice versa if a new student arrives). But anyway, that's not the point: the oasis has a "public search" functionality - also the API giving you all information about (meta)data without an account. This "public sharing" feature is nice and good for the official NOMAD instance, but not adequate for "data sharing between several groups in a protected environment". I want to restrict the API call and "gui/search" functionality only known users to promoted "sharing (not publishing) with the Collaborative Research Centre".

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: Does this help what I am aiming for?

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: To rephrase it: Despite the idea of NOMAD, I want to limit the access/visibility of all data within the oasis only to some chosen members/accounts and "misuse" the oasis as data sharing platform for dedicated scientist... That's the reason I change the source code, see my first post.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: My point is that with allowed_users set, you cannot access that "public search" without login.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text:
How for the API, I don't know
At least not that I am aware
The api also returns: `"detail": "Authentication is required for this Oasis"`
Our group's oasis runs exactly like this, with our user base in the allowed_users list.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text:
If the user base gets too large, having the possibility to restrict to any logged in user (from a custom key_cloak) would indeed be nice to have.
👍  Thank you!

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: Now, I understand why `allowed_users` for an oasis should to the same thing.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: I've not be aware of, that this also will restrict the API call. I will check that in the file `nomad/app/v1/routers/auth.py` But as you facing the same problem as I am, maybe a more general approach without relying on nomad.yaml would be great.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text:
Thanks again for the advice 🙂
Sounds like  `oasis.require_authentication` could then be a feature to implement. This would be something that could be a less strict alternative to the `allowed_users`. If this setting is enabled, we basically would treat non-logged in users as though they are not in the `allowed_users` list.
☺️ Yes, I would appreciate that 👍
sounds also very useful to me
Alright!

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text: We will see how easy/hard the change is.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
text:
But to me this sounds like something we can put into the next release which is planned for april/may.
@Lauri_Himanen (CC to @Laurenz_Rettig ): As it seems [not so easy anymore](https://discord.com/channels/[REDACTED_PHONE]/[REDACTED_PHONE]) to directly rewrite the source code and create a new docker image, is there any progress/consideration with NOMAD Oasis v1.3.16? I do not see any changes in the [documentation](https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/oasis/admin.html#restricting-access-to-your-oasis).

title: Publishing an entry from Oasis to Central NOMAD
text:
I have installed an Oasis locally on my computer and I would like to push an published entry on my Oasis to the central NOMAD. I used the button in the upload page but a message says that `"Only admin of Oasis can publish to the central NOMAD. (401)"`
I assumed that as I installed it, I was some sort of admin of this Oasis but it is not the case and it is ok. I went to the "Perform admin tasks" page in the documentation but it doesn't seem to explain how to do that for now. Then, I wanted to know more about how it works and its final result in the central NOMAD. I recall that after following the tutorial 13, I asked a question about that mechanism (publish from an Oasis to central NOMAD) and I was told that it was not fully functional at the moment. In the Add More Power project, we should have soon a functional Oasis that will welcome the data of the project and we have to make some of them public, that's why I am interested about it.
1. As of now, is it possible (as admin) to publish a complete entry from an Oasis to central NOMAD using the dedicated button or do we have to go directly in central NOMAD and make upload there ?

title: Publishing an entry from Oasis to Central NOMAD
text: Then, I will take the opportunity to do it right now ! I think I have part of the answer to it but I prefer to discuss this. As suggested by the third point in the 'Current Limitations' section of the documentation mentioned above, only a metadata transfer could happen at some point, to avoid having to move big raw files. I guess that to do this would be easier if the metadata sections concerned have the same "environment" (the same available metadata fields) between both the Oasis and the central NOMAD, for instance, the `results` section. However, when it comes to having a new parser in the Oasis, once the parsing is made, the resulting entry already use the NOMAD Metainfo language, because the parser is designed this way and so it shares the same language with the central NOMAD. So my question here: how is it different (if it really is) in this case to transfer the metadata to the central NOMAD, compared to only the `results` section let's say ? Part of my answer with my understanding: Even if new metadata fields can be integrated in an Oasis through the bespoke schemas and parsers, they don't exist in the central NOMAD and so it is not just a matter of being written in the NOMAD Metainfo language to be able to go the central NOMAD but in addition, the central NOMAD must already have these metadata fields available somewhere (just like the `results` section). Just like one can upload data with a JSON file with data formatted under the NOMAD Metainfo language and a YAML schema, the schema says NOMAD which metadata to create and the JSON which value to fill which field with. So having a complete entry in an Oasis exported as JSON would be having the JSON file containing the data in the Metainfo language but still, the central NOMAD would still miss the YAML schema to know how to integrate it to its database, right ? I hope the question and the part explanation are not so blurry...

title: Publishing an entry from Oasis to Central NOMAD
text: Hi, @Wilfried_Hortschitz !

title: Publishing an entry from Oasis to Central NOMAD
text: So on my side, I am developing basic parsers (for now) for multiple experimental techniques (EBSD, TEM, ECCI, SXDM...), each of the parsers being related to a technique (and so a specific output format). The Oasis used in the project I am using is private, so not possible to mint DOIs on the published datasets in the Oasis but still, we have to make some datasets public and so to publish them in central NOMAD. As the publish link between an Oasis and central NOMAD is not possible yet, I found a "manual workaround". When I am developing the parser+plugin for a technique, I create the Python schema alongside its counterpart in YAML (I write the file manually). Once the job is done in Python, I find it rather straightforward to write it in YAML. The idea here is to plug the parser+schema on my Oasis with the Python, parse the files when uploading them to the Oasis and then export the entry in a JSON format. This way, I have a YAML file for the schema and the JSON file for the data that has been created automatically via the parsing, so I don't have to write the JSON myself. Then, I am using the YAML and JSON files to upload to central NOMAD. This is a bit manual but I assume that developing a parser means that the data file format is not recognised by core parsers and so they will not be recognised when uploaded (at least, this is my case). With the YAML and JSON, I can then upload my unrecognised files and create the entry with the data contained in the JSON file, which act as a descriptive tag for the unrecognised files.

title: Publishing an entry from Oasis to Central NOMAD
text:
However, when doing so, I need to point to the schema file in the JSON file with the `m_def` metadata to reference the YAML file with `"m_def": "../uploads/{id}/raw/my_schema.archive.yaml#SectionName"` (if the schema file is in an other upload, following https://support.hdfgroup.org/documentation/hdf5-docs/advanced_topics/intro_SWMR.html)
However !

But when I upload all the files on central NOMAD, the YAML and JSON files are well processed and it only misses the final go from the scientists. So not fully tested but still, the entries are created without errors for now !

title: Pydantic 2.11 is breaking nomad
text: Down grading to <2.11 solved it

title: Pydantic validation errors in QueryEditQuantity
text:
Recently, when combining the filters for a query in `QueryEditQuantity`, the processing leads to Pydantic validation errors. Here's what happens:
- In the ELN entry, I use a quantity defined with the `QueryEditQuantity` annotations to build a query via the search dialog box.
- I create a search using two filters, let's say `entry_type = JupyterAnalysis` and `upload_id = <hash>`
- When I save the entry, the quantity is processed successfully
- However, if I save the entry again, the processing breaks, stating Pydantic validation errors.

title: Pydantic validation errors in QueryEditQuantity
text:
Here's an example: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis/gui/user/uploads/upload/id/fnyfkrg6ROa7DKX9zagsTw/entry/id/cNGZ4fYfxbV19OiogeJQtRUqjGmS/logs

I have made an upload on the example oasis for this issue: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis/gui/user/uploads/upload/id/fnyfkrg6ROa7DKX9zagsTw/.

title: Pydantic validation errors in QueryEditQuantity
text: There are two entries: one was saved only once, **`first_processing.archive.yaml`**, while the other **`second_processing.archive.yaml`** was saved again, which led to breaking it.

title: Pydantic validation errors in QueryEditQuantity
text:
Error is available in the logs of the broken entry. In the provided upload, one can see in the processed query of the `second_processing` entry, the `and` key is modified to `op`. I think this is what fails the validation.
- mainfile of the `first_processing` entry
- main file of the `second_processing` entry:
@ahmed_ilyas and I were trying to debug.

title: Pydantic validation errors in QueryEditQuantity
text: Maybe @Lauri_Himanen can help us identify the issue?

title: Pydantic validation errors in QueryEditQuantity
text: So the validation done [here](https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/datamodel/data.py?ref_type=heads#L267) is failing.

title: Pydantic validation errors in QueryEditQuantity
text:
Fixing this would be a matter of tracking down which part of the code is modifyig the query in the second processing run: My guess would be that the `model_validata(value).model_dump()` is creating this malformed version during the first processing. I found the issue. In the models for operators, the name of the field is `op` while the actual name of the operator is defined as an alias (for example [here](https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/app/v1/models/models.py?ref_type=heads#L118) ). When `model_dump()` is used, it defaults to using `op` as the key. Setting `model_dump(by_alias=True)` fixes the issue
Fixed here https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/merge_requests/2493

title: Quantities with experimental uncertainties
text:
Is there currently a NOMAD way of saving a experimental measurement uncertainty assigned to a Quantity? (Eg. mean value +- std)
If no, I would imagine this will be useful for everyone handling experimental data.

title: Quantities with experimental uncertainties
text:
Not yet, but on the PhysicalProperty discussion we are talking about these statistics
For now we are adding a second quantity next to it.

title: Quantities with experimental uncertainties
text:
See for example: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-material-processing/blob/main/src/nomad_material_processing/general.py#L573
My recommendation is: you can try it out now yourself, either following @Hampus_Näsström and Area A implementation, or doing it more abstract, following Area C current def of PhysicalProperty (https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-simulations/blob/develop/src/nomad_simulations/schema_packages/physical_property.py#L69).

title: Quantities with experimental uncertainties
text: Bear in mind: 1. the proper implementation of `PhysicalProperty` might take some weeks/months still, 2. whatever you implement now it should be then refactor afterwards into this new structure.

title: Quantities with experimental uncertainties
text: I'd say 2nd option is more appealing from my point of view, as it will directly reflect on implementing something practical on the interoperability TF, and it will allow you to be ready to refactor more consistently and easy for you. First option might mean you need to change a lot of logic here and there.

title: Quantities with experimental uncertainties
text:
I think @Markus_Scheidgen had some slides with a suggestion for statistics under a PhysicalProperty
Thank you guys.

title: Quantities with experimental uncertainties
text: Fortunately, it's not an urgent problem for me, but it came up in more than one conversation, so I was curious.

title: Quantities with experimental uncertainties
text:
Note that experimental datasets in NOMAD parsed from NeXus are following the NeXus convention (similar to what Hampus showed):
Errors or uncertainties on a data quantity DATA__field are stored in the DATA_errors__field
You can see it in the Ellipsometry example upload how it is represented in NeXus and also in NOMAD.

title: RAM etc requirements for installing an Oasis
text: Hi, after having worked for some months on a local Oasis installed on my office computer, I am now trying to install an Oasis on my laptop, in view of my participation to the upcoming FAIRmat users meeting in Bochum on Jul. 2-3.

title: RAM etc requirements for installing an Oasis
text: The general requirements for installing an Oasis are often indicated as at least 8 Gb RAM and at least 4 CPUs. Indeed my laptop has 4 CPUs, but as for the total RAM it shows: mem: 3.7 Gb, swap: 2 Gb. I have tried anyway (following the general instructions on how to install an Oasis... which had worked on my office pc, with 6 CPUs and 7.5 mem and 5.4 swap as for the RAM), and everything seems to be ok, up to the command "uv run poe setup" included.

title: RAM etc requirements for installing an Oasis
text:
Then, issuing "uv run poe start", my laptop gets stuck, i.e. it does not proceed after                                             [tasks]
  . nomad.app.resources.routers.resources.retrieve_resources
  . nomad.processing.base.proc_task
  . nomad.processing.base.transfer_logs                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ...and ceases to respond to any other commands (the only way out being a restart).

title: RAM etc requirements for installing an Oasis
text: I can imagine this is due to not having enough RAM...?

title: RAM etc requirements for installing an Oasis
text: In that case, is there some workaround in order to be able to run the Oasis GUI on that device?   (I have just checked - with 1 test only - that the Oasis at least runs from command line,  without GUI,  i.e.  uv run nomad parse ......).

title: RAM etc requirements for installing an Oasis
text: Thanks for any suggestions!

title: RAM etc requirements for installing an Oasis
text: Hi Elena, you could try using the --dev flag for appworker command. `uv run nomad admin run appworker --dev`.

title: RAM etc requirements for installing an Oasis
text: That should reduce the number of workers used.

title: Re-parsing of (old) archives with updated parsers
text:
Hi all, based on my own recent experience both in using NOMAD for searching and visualizing data and in implementing new features (see my other posts in /issues and in /forum-comp), and also based on discussions with colleagues, we think it would be very desirable / useful to have NOMAD periodically (even not too often, let's say once a year or so) make an "automatic re-parsing" of all its archives / uploads (or possibly only of those older than XXX...some criterion), to ensure that data archived on NOMAD have been parsed with reasonably up-to-date parsers. As far as I understand, this seems not to be the case, currently, so that on NOMAD there are some "old" archives which have been parsed at the time of their upload e.g. 10 years ago, therefore with old parsers, lacking several functionalities that were not present at that time and have been developed in the meantime, and therefore reporting/displaying/plotting only a subset of the data that would be plotted by up-to-date parsers (see also
https://discord.com/channels/[REDACTED_PHONE]/[REDACTED_PHONE]/threads/[REDACTED_PHONE]): is this correct? This situation entails a lack of visibility of potentially available data and metadata when making queries, a lack of "internal coherence" (more or less complete sets of data shown, depending on the "age" of the upload)  and is also rather confusing for users aiming at contributing with developments (one is never sure of which of the functionalities present in a parser really work correctly, until one makes a new upload of data from a given code).

title: Re-parsing of (old) archives with updated parsers
text: Is this periodic re-parsing of archives something you are considering?

title: Re-parsing of (old) archives with updated parsers
text: It seems to me an issue of rather general interest, and I can imagine several users have already asked for this, so probably there are reasons for not having implemented it..?

title: Re-parsing of (old) archives with updated parsers
text: Or has it been implemented at least partially, and perhaps I have just run into some "pathological" archives that had not been re-parsed?

title: Re-parsing of (old) archives with updated parsers
text: Thanks for any explanations. Hi @Elena_Molteni, thanks for reaching out about this.

title: Re-parsing of (old) archives with updated parsers
text: I think something like 1.5 years ago we tried a relatively high scale reparsing effort.

title: Re-parsing of (old) archives with updated parsers
text: This unfortunately, it is not a fully automate process that we can trigger periodically, and experience showed us that sometimes there are uploads that have entries that show some parsing errors.

title: Re-parsing of (old) archives with updated parsers
text: This typically leads to further fixes in the parsers and triggering the reprocessing again. I do agree though that this might be something to explore in the near future on how to automate these kind of pipelines.

title: Re-parsing of (old) archives with updated parsers
text: I will make note of this for our future plans.

title: Re-parsing of (old) archives with updated parsers
text: In the meantime, are there certain uploads or datasets that you would find particularly important for you to be reprocessed?

title: Recreating deleted upload on OASIS
text: Do we need to restore the mongodb if we want to reintroduce a deleted upload? Or would it be enough to reintroduce the folder in the .volumes and trigger a reprocessing from the CLI?

title: Recreating deleted upload on OASIS
text: Depends: MongoDB does contain information that cannot be recreated by simply reprocessing (upload id, entry id, timestamps, authors, coauthors, inclusion in datasets, etc).

title: Recreating deleted upload on OASIS
text: If you need to restore this, you will need the MongoDB snapshot. But if you don't care, you can simply reprocess.

title: Recreating deleted upload on OASIS
text: Hrmm, okay, I guess the main issue is if there are references to this upload and we then create a new one. Is there a way to recreate and upload with a specified ID? The way to recreate everythign with an existing ID would be to move the correctly named folder into .volumes, re-add the data (uploads + entries) into MongoDB from a backup, and run `nomad admin uploads index <upload-id>` to get ES populated with data about the entries.

title: Referencing PlotSection: Error "The item "figures:0" could not be found."
text:
Hi NOMAD-Team,
I would appreciate any hint to avoid the error `The item "figures:0" could not be found.` by referencing a class with `PlotSection` from a different class (with NOMAD Oasis v1.3.14). The use case: I develop a "Overview-Class/Dashboard" for a list of (custom) "`ELNMeasurements`", which inherit the class `PlotSection` for depicting the results.

title: Referencing PlotSection: Error "The item "figures:0" could not be found."
text: An exemplary minimal example is attached.

title: Referencing PlotSection: Error "The item "figures:0" could not be found."
text: The nice feature and possibility to use the eln-annotation `overview: True` for sections to be displayed on a different entry allow to show the plots and works fine. But in the overview page the button `View data in the archive` in the PlotSection results in an error `The item "figures:0" could not be found.` as this seems to expect a list of figures in the "dashboard/overview" class and not directly referencing the figures in derived class.

title: Referencing PlotSection: Error "The item "figures:0" could not be found."
text:
Is there any way to adjust the code?

```
class MeasurementExample(ELNMeasurement, PlotSection, ArchiveSection):
    '''
    Class with PlotSection also shown in OverviewClass.
    '''
    m_def = Section(
        a_eln={
            "overview": True,
        },
        )
    
    def normalize(self, archive: 'EntryArchive', logger: 'BoundLogger'):
        # Otherwise create plot
        fig = px.scatter(x=[0, 1, 2, 3, 4], y=[0, 1, 4, 9, 16])
        
        self.figures = []
        self.figures.append(PlotlyFigure(label='Measurement Example:', figure=fig.to_plotly_json()))
        super().normalize(archive, logger)

class OverviewClass(EntryData, ArchiveSection):
    '''
    Class for overview of measurement section.
    '''
    m_def = Section()
    
    Measurement_type = Quantity(
        type=MEnum(['NMR', 'XRD', 'other']),
        a_eln={
            "component": "RadioEnumEditQuantity"
        },
    )

title: Remove default search apps and views
text:
I think the easiest is to instead only include the entry points you want. I think there is some way to exclude/include with wildcards but I'm afraid this is not documented very well. I think these apps are still there for historical reasons from before the plugin mechanism was developed. They are defined in the source code (`nomad/config/defaults.yaml`)
I am not entirely sure how to deactivate them, but I think you can add a nomad.yaml file with some specification. Sorry I'm late to the discussion.

title: Remove default search apps and views
text: As Julia mentioned, we have a few default apps that are installed by default on every OASIS, and they are defined in nomad/config/defaults.yaml.

title: Remove default search apps and views
text: If you want to disable these, you can either exclude them, or then only include the ones you want.

title: Remove default search apps and views
text:
Here is an example of both approaches using `nomad.yaml`:

```yaml
# Exclude
plugins:
  entry_points:
    exclude:
      - "apps/1_all/1_entries"
      - "apps/2_theory/1_calculations"
      - "apps/2_theory/2_materials"
      - "apps/3_experiment/1_eln"
      - "apps/3_experiment/2_eels"
```

```yaml
# Include
plugins:
  entry_points:
    include:
      - "my_plugin.apps:my_app"`
```

This assumes that you have a relative new OASIS version: let me know if it does not work and I can check which version you need.

title: Remove default search apps and views

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text:
If Entry is inheriting from ActivityStep, its rendering in the Data Browser raises a TypeError of sectionDef is undefinied
@Lauri_Himanen do you have any idea, what could go wrong?

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: Could be again that they are not `Schema`?

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: Or is it already `Schema `or `EntryData` in an upper level?

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text:
Yes, NeXus is a Schema
What is put into data
This Schema allows having NeXusMeasurements (e.g.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: Mpes) which are Measurements with an extra normalisation that sets the method used. The NexusMeasurements have Entries which are ActivitySteps.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: Note that I am normalising our Measurements so that `NexusMeasurement.steps`  (inherited from `Measurement.steps`) hold the different ActivitySteps.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: In this particular case, there are 2 of them, both are of type `Mpes.ENTRY` which is subclass of `Entry` that is subclass of `ActivityStep` (as shown on the first screenshot).

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text:
Without this normalisation, they render properly and show the ActivityStep related features, like `step name`:
Here, there are no ActivityStep-s registered below `steps`, but as soon as I add a specialised one, I got the error above. Talking to Andrea, we have found some working examples os steps e.g. as tey are used by Experiment BaseSection and its subclasses:

For https://github.com/PDI-Berlin/pdi-nomad-plugin/blob/d68e1c408ec6c6ffdfdc6c7b5703f093e9cbac6b/src/pdi_nomad_plugin/mbe/processes.py#L1185, I have some notes

    Activity.steps are defined as a Subsection (of ActivityStep-s) https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/datamodel/metainfo/basesections.py?ref_type=heads#L352
    Experiment.steps inherited from Activity.steps is redefined with the use of ExperimentStep as section_def. https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/datamodel/metainfo/basesections.py?ref_type=heads#L544

After an init_metainfo call (or during the assignment?), the corresponding python class may probably be fixed by adding (and/or) overriding the extra Quantities as class properties (activity, and lab_id).

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: Note that activity is a Quantity of type Activity and not of ActivityReference(!).

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: Also note that although ExperimentStep.lab_id is overriding BaseSection.lab_id, so its eln label becomes different, but a self.lab_id of an instance will still be assumed to be the same as BaseSection.lab_id (e.g. by the BaseSection.normalize method).

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: In the above code, ExperimentMbePDI.steps inherited from Experiment.steps is overwritten by a Subsection of ActivityReference-s.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: This means that ANY inherited functionality (e.g.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: Experiment.normalize) which may use self.steps will still assume that these are ActivityStep-s (or ExperimentStep-s), but certainly not that these are ActivityReference-s.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text:
I think, these kind of overrides can cause lots of confusions in the python code and potential errors in functionality, but might give hints to the GUI problem which is not implemented in python. Note that if I use ActivityStep directly and not a subclass, than the GUI seems to work:
Note the difference on how the GUI receives the data:
I'm sorry @Sandor_Brockhauser but I can't really follow your problem.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: We are specializing the `steps` subsection with specialized `ActivityStep` section throughout all our schemas.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: I don't really follow what the issue is.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: Maybe you have to explain it to me over a call.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: I was able to reproduce the problem locally using some MPES data as an example.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: My initial guess is that this is related to the fact that `pynxtools.nomad.schema.Mpes.ENTRY` is not a "regular" section definition, but seems to be what is called an "inner section definition" (`ENTRY` is defined within the `Mpes` class). Is this the case, @Sandor_Brockhauser? I can try and see if there is a way to fix this.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: What was the original reason for introducing inner section definitions in NeXus, and does that still apply? If it is possible to implement the NeXus schema without inner sections definitions, we should do it. @Hampus_Näsström Does Area A use inner section definitions?

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: This feature is not even documented at the moment really, there is only one example in `examples/metainfo/advanced_concepts.py`.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text:
No, as far as I know we don't use any inner section definitions.
@Sandor_Brockhauser: There is now a MR where I introduced a fix: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/merge_requests/2259.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: Could you test it out?

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: Before we merge that, we will need to also test that MR with some other complicated archives to make sure that I did not break anything in other schemas.

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text: Thank you for the fix!

title: Rendering an ActivityStep
text:
I have tested with an MPES and also with an    Ellipsometry example and worked fine there is no more a problem with the rendering.
vvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
so the code now works as expected

title: Reproducibility indicators bug from afm nexus file
text: Essentially, in `/NXafm/ENTRY/reproducibility_indicators`, we only define `cantilever_oscillator`, but none of its sub-items.

title: Reproducibility indicators bug from afm nexus file
text: NOMAD will then only try to parse the `cantilever_oscillator` group from the link, but not anything that is inside that Hdf5 group.

title: Reproducibility indicators bug from afm nexus file
text: This is the general case for links, if you want to have all sub-elements also show up at the link destination, you need to explicitly define them in application definition as well.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text:
Hey
if a nomad quantity is of dtype float it might contain `nan` values.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text: These are stored as `null` in the `archive.json`.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text: Then parsing the archive leads to an error, see attached.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text: This errors is caused by trying to cast an numpy object array containing `None`values to the numpy float dtype because of safe casting.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text: Can you have a look?

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text: I think @Theodore_Chang_DM_BITTE would know most about this.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text:
I guess we should allow null values to be read in as NaN. I guess JSON does not have NaN primitive.
i talked to theodore already
in python json works well with nan
how do you create the json which you write on disc
@Lauri_Himanen
theodore says, if json doesnt store it doesnt work, that would be quite an issue
What do you mean with "in Python JSON works well with NaN?" Officially the JSON specs don't contain anything for NaN: https://www.json.org/json-en.html

I think the problem might be solvable by having an implicit rule that any `null` value for a numeric quantity gets turned into `NaN` when deserializing. But this is where we need Theodore's opinion.
i emant that this works:
```python
a = {"ab":np.array([1,2,3,np.nan]).tolist()}
r = np.array(json.loads(json.dumps(a))["ab"])[-1]
type(r)
```
outputs `numpy.float64` and `json.dumps` outputs: `'{"ab": [1.0, 2.0, 3.0, NaN]}'`
so in python json store it as NaN but maybe that is not comliant with the general json specs
ah yes it says here: `

json.dumps has an allow_nan parameter, which defaults to True.`
I see. Since we use the JSON archives also in Javascript, I think we have to use fully JSON spec compliant output.
ah i see, then i will go back to theodore who was not so open to relaxing the strict type checking
since also `inf` is not permitted
i am fearing that when updating to a newer nomad version with this strict typsetting will create plenty of issues
right now i am still running on a version from before that
if then all `null` values will break we can not update
Come on @Lauri_Himanen and @Theodore_Chang_DM_BITTE we can't have it like this! We should just relax the "type checking" so that `null` becomes `nan`.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text: This becomes a much larger issue when the front end is involved in the picture. Could you @Micha create an issue about this in Gitlab, and we continue the discussion there.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text: I think the only clean solution would be to maybe have some flavor of JSON that supports NaN and Infs, but this might not be so easy.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text:
Just converting `NaN` and `Inf` to `null` will not do as that leads to information loss.
i can create an issue and tag it here
https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/2128
If null is nan what about inf
What have we done until you introduced the breaking changes?

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text: We can't have the processing of old archives break on existing oases. The we need to put some sort of check for backwards compatibility and treat that as we have doen until now.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text:
The ideal solution would be if non editable quantites are never written to disc but always loaded or setby the normalizer
it doesnt make sense anyhow to store a non editable quantity whihc is derived from raw data into json
then on load it is always np.nan and np.inf
Ah, i have been facing this issue and scratching my head all along.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text: I am glad that it's a known issue.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text: The error leads to the entire data section not being found, making it hard to find the problem.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text:
We need slightly better error handling here
I don't think that we have a solution here.

title: Safe type casting causes error converting json null to numpy float
text: I am not even sure if there is concensus on the issue and the scope of the problem. I tried to categorize the problem space a bit.

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text: The old gui does not check what has been modified in NOMAD ELN by user, rather suggests pushing the **whole entry** to be saved in the mainfile.

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text: This case problem, because lots of information should **not be** part of the main file, but rather derived during its parsing and normalisation.

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text: As a result, functions those create such derived information shall be very careful.

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text:
See the example in the Activity base section:
```
if self.method:
  archive.results.eln.methods.append(self.method)
else:
  archive.results.eln.methods.append(self.m_def.name)
if archive.workflow2 is None:
  archive.workflow2 = Workflow(name=self.name)
archive.workflow2.tasks = [step.to_task() for step in self.steps]
```
Note that results.eln.methods list is appended, but workflow2.tasks list is overwritten.
`results` seem to be the place where normalisation is expected to output, so it is **not saved** to the mainfile. On the other hand, `workflow2` is expected to be provided directly, so it is saved to and then fetched from the mainfile. The problem is that Activity (and other Base Sections) do not follow the rule, but instead, their normalisation functions output to workflow2. How do they handle the situation that some other sources may have dropped here something what these base sections were not prepared to? They simply remove everything from here (independent if it was a useful thing or not) and populate the workflow2 with their own stuff. In this way, even 2 base section cannot coexists in an Archive (since one will remove the output of the another one), but it cannot coexists with anything else which would like to serve information to workflow2.

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text: This results, that every base section must stay alone in its own private Entry.

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text: What should have happened instead?

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text: A Workflow Card could handle workflows from the results section. So **any** normalisation which would like to produce a workflow for being displayed, it could drop its derived workflow to results where its expected place is.

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text: Which is by the way not effected by the GUI bug.

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text: Changing base sections to normalise to `archive.results.workflow2` is a quick fix. Making a Workflow Card which picks up the data from `archive.results.workflow2` and not (or not only) from `archive.workflow2` is also quick and easy.

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text:
Any counterarguments?
@Andrea_Albino @Markus_Scheidgen @Lauri_Himanen ?

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text: Hi, yes this problem needs indeed to be solved.

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text:
We could address this in the MR: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/2053

More thoughts on that are that we were discussing about a Normalizer plugin that is in charge of filling Results section, and so one can think to a Workflow normalizer, too. I think we discussed this option some time ago when discussing with Lauri and Hampus about the possible weakness of a normalize method that simoultaneously touches the Data, Results, and Workflow. Having separate normalizer creates a separation in the steps of the various archive section filling in.

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text: I agree with Andrea.

title: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
text:
Let's deal with the overwriting when we tackle the refactoring of the basesection normalize methods. Let me mark this base section problem as a bug, because this forbids the generation of the workflow in our experiment entries. While thinking about a complete redesign and implementing it, we also need a quick fix which demonstrates the the proper workflow generation as something provided by and inherited from the basesections. Alternatively, we could just implement the correct behaviour in new classes which DO NOT inherit normalisation from base sections, but the whole point in using base sections is that the functionalities are inherited. Changed the channel name: Saving bug in old GUI; normalisation to workflow2
