title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: @Lauri_Himanen When I try to focus on a specific region in a scatterplot in the catalysis app, the filter does not get updated in the same way as I can see it does in the production of the Solar Cells app. Both regarding the update of the range in the window but also regarding the displayed entry list. I think this is related to the repeating subsections and array quantities, but I am not sure if this is the intended behaviour or a bug. Can I tests this in the Oasis?

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: Which scatter plot?

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: Note that when you select a region in the scatter plot, you are essentially setting a filter like `500 K < temperature < 600 K AND 50 < conversion < 80`.

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: If `temperature` is an array, this will include entries where temperature is e.g. [300, 400, 500].

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: But maybe first let me know how to reproduce this and then we can see if there is indeed a bug, or if the behaviour is simply unexpected and should be reconsidered.

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text:
Yes, you can test this in the[Test Oasis](https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis/gui/search/heterogeneouscatalyst?results.properties.catalytic.reaction.reaction_conditions.temperature%5Bgte%5D=[REDACTED_PHONE]%20K&results.properties.catalytic.reaction.reaction_conditions.temperature%5Blte%5D=[REDACTED_PHONE]%20K)
it does show the first filter, e.g. for the temperature, but not the other axis, e.g. the feed composition, it also does not update the range that it shows in the widget. partly it is because an entry that falls within the search window also has data points outside.
but there is some problem for the 2nd axis, probably because it is not specified further , e.g.reactants[*].conversion
so it looks like it does not attempt any filtering along the 2nd axis
Yes, looks like somehow the filter for one of the axis is not set correctly, that is definitely a bug.

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: Any idea what we should do in the case where we filter for a value in an array? Maybe we keep all the entries that contain at least one match in the array, but we should simply zoom in on the region and not show the rest of the points that did not match. Or do you think we should require all of the points in that array to match the criterion? Yes, the first would be the desired effect. Keep all entries that have at least one point in the desired region. But zoom in.

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: Thanks!

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: I was able to fix the bug, but I think I cannot create a very good solution for the other problem. I think we have to separate the current "Focus on region" into two different actions: "Zoom" and "Filter". "Zoom" will simply zoom in on the selected region without affecting any of the filters. "Filter" would create filters from the current selection, and if "autorange=true", the plotted region changes accordingly. The reason why the "Filter" results may seem weird is the fact that filters simply return all entries where there is some data that fullfills the query. It does not care about where in the data the condition is filled, and will always just return the full entry. This is why if you create a filter based on `results.properties.catalytic.reaction.reaction_conditions.temperature` `results.properties.catalytic.reaction.reactants.gas_concentration_in`, the plot may also contain other reactions, simply because they are included in this entry where one of the reactions matched the query. If we want, we could try to anticipate these unexpected results by disabling the "Filter" action completely when dealing with non-scalar data. You would still be able to zoom in on the data.

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: I think I understand.

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: Do you think it would be possible to update the legend (marker) to just include the labels for those entries with points in the focused region (Filter or Zoom)?

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: If I want to use `results.properties.catalytic.catalyst.catalyst_name` as the marker.

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: That would be very helpful.

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
text: Let me see what I can do

title: Search in data section
text: What I think is happening is that here: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/pynxtools/blob/master/src/pynxtools/nomad/schema.py#L836 where the section for `pynxtools.nomad.schema.NeXus` is defined, a regular `Section` is being used, whereas it should be an instance of  `Schema` (`from nomad.datamodel.data import Schema`).

title: Search in data section
text: Only data from schemas is possible to load into search apps.

title: Search in data section
text: I would assume that after this is changed, you could load the definitions into an app with something like: `search_quantities=SearchQuantities(include=['*#pynxtools.nomad.schema.NeXus])`.

title: Search in data section
text:
Please note that some fields are actually searchable:
but not in the general search line:
data.Mpes.datetime#pynxtools.nomad.schema.NeXus
After a long day, I could finally make the necessary modifications for making `pynxtools.nomad.schema.NeXus` to be a Schema as you suggested. Th changes are actually minimal, but I had to spend quite some time to understand the structure and how definitions are built during import from the python classes, so we can do the exact opposite (first the definitions and than the classes) just like in case of creating the schema from yaml. The actual code which indeed required this change is here in `reload_quantities_dynamic`: `elasticsearch_extension.py line 530`:
```
if isinstance(section, Section) and issubclass(
                    section.section_cls, EntryData
                ):
```
This code only takes sections of a package (and their schema tree) if the Section is also an EntryData,

Note that this function is called 15 times(!) during an appworker startup and each times, it walks through the whole tree of all these sections.

title: Search in data section
text: Without NeXus, we have only 815 search quantities here, so it might not be a big deal, but when we want to search for thousands of quantities, it is probably worth optimising it a bit. Also note that in this code, we simply check quantities independently and create corresponding search_quantities.

title: Search in data section
text: Next to avoid unnecessary traverses, this is also an ideal case for parallelisation.

title: Search in data section
text: Currently, a single core is used and this results in a long startup if lots of search_qauntities are to be registered.

title: Specialized Scatterplots with JMESPath syntax
text:
Sorry, I have another question related to scatterplots @Lauri_Himanen : 
Is it expected that the elemental composition from results/material cannot be used as a marker in a scatterplot? I am trying to get a plot with the mass_fraction of a certain element as marker color in a scatterplot, but it doesn't seem to work. I can use e.g. `results.material.n_elements`  or use a label e.g. `results.material.elements[-1]`  but if I try to use `results.material.elemental_composition[0].element` I get as marker "undefined" and if I change from `element` to `mass_fraction` I get an empty plot.

title: Specialized Scatterplots with JMESPath syntax
text: Although those properties are filled for those entries in the results section.

title: Specialized Scatterplots with JMESPath syntax
text: I was able to use `results.material.elemental_composition[0].element` as a marker color.

title: Specialized Scatterplots with JMESPath syntax
text: I will send some screenshots.

title: Specialized Scatterplots with JMESPath syntax
text:
Your issue could be caused by some of the following reasons:
 - Scatter plots only sample up to a certain size (whatever you have defined in the scatter plot setup), and if none of these samples have this data set, you will only see `undefined`.
- Maybe you have some additional filters that prevent the data from entering the scatter plot?
- Maybe your choice of x/y axes is contradictory with the marker color (maybe `results.material.elemental_composition[0].element` is not set whenever your x/y value is set?)
Thanks a lot for your reply.

Standard way of writing `m_proxy_value`
text:
In the nomad-lab codebase, I can find two different ways of writing the m_proxy_value
```xml
<entry_path>#<section_path>
<entry_path>#/<section_path> (with a forward slash)
```
This also appears in practice, when I create a reference using ReferenceEditQuantity in the GUI: `reference.m_proxy_value` is of the form `<entry_path>#<section_path>`. However, after I save the entry, the value gets modified into the form  `<entry_path>#/<section_path>`. The change happens somewhere in the  `MetainfoNormalizer` 

Is there some reason for this discrepancy?

title: Standard way of writing `m_proxy_value`
text: Can we stick with one form?

title: Standard way of writing `m_proxy_value`
text:
I came across this because I am comparing the `m_proxy_values` to identify identical references, and because of this discrepancy, I get different results before and after the `MetainfoNormalizer` is run. I have also had issues with this in the past and would very much appreciate if we stuck to one way of writing this.
@Theodore_Chang_DM_BITTE can you comment on this. I think the with `/` is the normalized form, right?

title: Standard way of writing `m_proxy_value`
text:
Not sure why the ReferenceEditQuantity is doing it differently. I think Theodore gave the opinion that it was the intended behavior which I still don't understand why. See the discussion in this issue: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/1368
From what I am reading here, I could imagine a ReferenceEditQuantity that is already using the form with `/` so that the normalisation does not have to add it later on.

title: Standard way of writing `m_proxy_value`
text: But how big of an issue is this?

title: Standard way of writing `m_proxy_value`
text: Not sure.

title: Standard way of writing `m_proxy_value`
text:
I haven't run into any problems recently but remembered a lot of headache from back when I wrote that issue 😅
in my function that compares the m_proxy_values (inside normalizer), I am adding / in the path before comparison.

title: Standard way of writing `m_proxy_value`
text: So for now, it works for as well. but it took some time to debug it given that one would not expect deviations in the form of the proxy value.

title: Standard way of writing `m_proxy_value`
text:
I am afraid someone might run into this again in future 😅
Adding a leading / is part of the normalization so that we deal with a canonical form.

title: Standard way of writing `m_proxy_value`
text: What is happening at the front end is beyond me.

title: Standard way of writing `m_proxy_value`
text:
This isn't super important at the moment, but maybe we can assign this issue to someone who is working in the frontend to work this out? 🙂
Could you create an issue on gitlab? @mohammad.nakhaee might know best how this works.

title: Standard way of writing `m_proxy_value`

title: Suggestions in Inputfields
text:
Hey, 

can someone tell me when the Terms WIdgets with Input fields allows suggestions based on already type information?

title: Suggestions in Inputfields
text:
Hi Micha, I believe this is only possible for those terms defined in the results.py file in the central software, which have an added property `a_elasticsearch =[Elasticsearch(suggestion='default'),]` as e.g. here:
```
name = Quantity(
        type=str,
        shape=[],
        description="""
        Name of the product, preferably the IUPAC name.
        """,
        a_elasticsearch=[
            Elasticsearch(material_entry_type),
            Elasticsearch(suggestion='default'),
        ],
    )
```
(I could be wrong, and there might be another way for terms from plugin definitions)
That would explain it thanks 🙂
is there a possibility to enable this for custom quantities?

title: Suggestions in Inputfields
text: Julia is correct.

title: Suggestions in Inputfields
text: We only collect suggestions for data stored in `results`.

title: Suggestions in Inputfields
text: Because the custom quantities use a different storage mechanism (which allows better scaling to multiple schemas + base class searches), adding suggestions to them does not work in the same way.

title: Suggestions in Inputfields
text: There is a technical solutions that would make this possible, but it will be much slower.

title: Suggestions in Inputfields
text: We are not prepared to explore this much further at this point in time.

title: Suggestions in Inputfields
text: Ok I understand.

title: Switch view between Data and Plot
text: Hi!

title: Switch view between Data and Plot
text:
If you are using `PlotSection` as documented here: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/reference/annotations.html#plot,  you should have a `figures` subsection in your data that contains the full plot data as JSON. You can see an example here: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/gui/search/solarcells/entry/id/t3X--o-QsCZ7EzUsA3gnvTOmQJTX/data/data/figures:0/figure

You can of also store the raw data for your plots in a separate quantity in your schema if you prefer having it in a more structured way.

title: Switch view between Data and Plot
text: It is quite typical that your Plots are actually generated from a combination of several quantities.

title: Tabular parser row chemical_formula
text: I need to parse a table in a xlsx or csv which holds information about the chemical_formula for many different samples (~300).

title: Tabular parser row chemical_formula

title: Tabular parser row chemical_formula
text:
I started with the example from here https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-docs/blob/b1b523de65183d937fbeb1398fead43a26c31fae/examples/data/docs/tabular-parser-row-mode.archive.yaml and extended the table in the xlsx to also have a column named 'chemical_formula' and holds values like 'Fe' or 'Co'. I also introduced  the corresponding base_sections for using `chemical_formular` from https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/docs/howto/customization/elns.html#example-eln-schema under My_Subsection:
```
          base_sections:
           - 'nomad.datamodel.metainfo.eln.Chemical'
           - 'nomad.datamodel.data.EntryData'  
```
but still `chemical_formular` it is not recognized or used than for providing chemical elements for this upload (please see the attached screenshot). I also attached the used yaml and xlsx files.

title: Tabular parser row chemical_formula
text: How should this be done?

title: Tabular parser row chemical_formula
Tabular parser row chemical_formula
text:
Thanks in advance
I only moved the docs from nomad-FAIR to nomad-docs, I think @Amir_Golparvar might be able to help here
Hi Wilfried,

I guess what you want to do is to have the `Chemical` base section to be in `My_Section` like this:

which would look like this:
@Amir_Golparvar thanks for the answer. I tried this.

title: Tabular parser row chemical_formula
text: In the DATA tab the chemical_formula is show correctly but one can not search under 'Publish'->'Search your data'->'Filter'->'Material'->'Elements' for it.

title: Tabular parser row chemical_formula
text: Oh that's a different issue.

title: Tabular parser row chemical_formula
text:
To search data from there, you need to fill different part of your schema, which is the `results` section. As I have shown in the video, the results section is only the one that makes your quantities searchable, and if you want to make the quantities under the `data` section searchable, we need to define a nomad `app`, which would take a lot of time. One solution in my opinion is to fill the results section with `nomad.datamodel.results.material` base class. @Lauri_Himanen or @Alvin_Noe_Ladines knows more about this.
@Wilfried_Hortschitz @Amir_Golparvar  I dont really understand why this approach with the chemical formula does not work. I would have thought that the basesection normalize function does already fill the `results.materials` section automatically if you inherit from it? I do something similar, directly with the `elemental_composition` subsection and it works with the tabular parser. You just have to have a column for every element. you can even add the atom fraction in extra columns in the same order, then you should get the same information as from chemical_formula?

title: Tabular parser row chemical_formula
text:

@Julia_Schumann Thanks for sharing the schema.

title: Tabular parser row chemical_formula

text: The class `nomad.datamodel.metainfo.eln.Chemical` does not have such normalizer.

title: Tabular parser row chemical_formula
text:
I am just not familiar with any basesections that contain such normalizers to populate the results section.
@Julia_Schumann @Amir_Golparvar Thank you very much. With your suggestions I managed to modify the original example and read a table with one element as you can see in the images below. What I do not understand how to modify the attached basic example .yaml and .xlsx (or .csv) to read multiple columns of elements.

title: Test scripts for schema package - parse function needs change?
text:
Hello,
My message is with regards to this PR - https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-schema-plugin-nmr/pull/1, where I am trying to add in some physical quantities validation to the tests to present example representations of each data class that the schema package defines. I have tried to build upon the test_schema_package.py file within the tests folder. I'm attaching the tests folder that I'm locally working on as a .zip file. The parse function from nomad.client packages the chemical symbol and index information in my input test file as a MProxy object, but the normalizer expects an AtomsState object for the data to be ingested correctly.

title: Test scripts for schema package - parse function needs change?
text: Could you help me improve the test code and/or my example files please?

title: Test scripts for schema package - parse function needs change?
text:
This is the only outstanding issue before I can close the PR. Many thanks. @Andrea_Albino @Lauri_Himanen @Sarthak_Kapoor @Esma_Boydas
For this PR, I am leaving out the feature that Esma is working on to resolve and name the atom site labels differently (for reference: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-schema-plugin-nmr/issues/12). I am okay with keeping the site labels as such in the original code for now, but require help with the test scripts. Hi Sathya, when sections are added as reference, they are composed with `MProxy` definition, which has a method to resolve the reference and replace `MProxy` with the actual section definition (`AtomsState` in your case). Can you try the following:
```py
from nomad.metainfo import MProxy
..

atom_state = <path to the instance>
if isinstance(atom_state, MProxy):
  atom_state.m_proxy_resolve()

# now access the quantity in the object
assert atom_state.my_quantity
```
Hi Sathya, i have added a `label` quantity under `AtomsState`, and please see my comment in the issue👌
Hi Esma,

Thank you for adding the label field to nomad-simulations so quickly. I have read your questions on the GH issue as well. I'll test the changes as advised by Sarthak first and get back to you as soon as I can this week. 🙂

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: I was wondering what the expierence with nomad updates is.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: When i am updating plugins reloads are not helping but we have to do strg+shift+R.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: Is it possible to force a proper reload of nomad also when plugins have changes? is someone else experiencing that?

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text:
There are two potential causes for things not updating. 
1.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: The actual GUI code is only updated when the GUI has changed. There is a hash based on the GUI used as the name for the Javascript file that the browser is loading.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: Those are basically cached indefinetely.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text:
But this should not be a problem, has the GUI code has not changed. 
2.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: The GUI uses a resource called `artifacts.js` and `env.js`.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: Those are dynamically generated by the nomad app at start-up time.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: The `artifacts.js` contains all the schemas and stuff.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: I assume this is what actually gives you trouble.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: There is caching on these, because they can be quite large. But the caching is supposed to be based on an "etag" which again is based on a hash of the `artifacts.js` content.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: This "etag" should change when you plugins change.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: Maybe something is wrong here.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: Your browsers network tab, you can see if a resource is loaded from cache or not.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text:
On the first reload after a change, you should see that the `artifacts.js` has actually been loaded from the server. In your network tab, you should see these response headers on the `artifacts.js`:
`Cache-Control: max-age=60, must-revalidate`
`Etag: W/"969f39d2e790f15232c0bbe87463fc6c"`
The `must-revalidate` tells the browser if the last cached result is older than 60 seconds it should go to the server. If this happens the Etag is compared. If the Etag has not changed, the backend will only respond with 304 Not Modified. If the Etag has changed (what should happen if you modified a plugin schema for example), it should return 200 including the actual content. I did a small update of the schema (hiding a quantity) it did not update until i clicked strg+ctrl+R the `etag` didnt change
Your screenshoots don't say if it was a cached result or a real one.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: Did you had a look?

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text:
You have to use this view
Here you can see if it is "(memory cache)" or not, it also shows you the return code, e.g. 304. If you open the `artifacts.js` alone (e.g. new tab with "https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/gui/artifacts.js") and if you wait for 60 seconds before sending, the network tab should always look like this:
Either it is 304 with a tiny size (etag did not change), or 200 with a large size (etag changed). If it says "(memory cache)" in the size column than you have not waited 60 s
Ok I have to test again then:)
The Etag was the same and only after refresh with shift it was displayed correctly
But I didn’t wait 60s
Would it then reload even without a simple reload (Strg+r)?

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: I dont get it.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: In the update i hide lab_id.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text:
Only the last reload is with strg+shift+r and then it is displayed correctly, but the ressources still says from cache. somethinge doesnt add up 😦
You really have to look at `artifacts.js` isolated in a separate tab.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: If you load the whole NOMAD GUI, it will load everything twice.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: Its so fast, you probably don't notice.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: With this, you will always see "Aus cache".

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: But this might just mean the version you just loaded a few milliseconds before.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: Anyhow,  the etag should really change when you change a python schema + update + 60s. So, if you are seeing the same etag, there is a problem. Does the etag change after your hard reload (shift+ctr+r)?

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: What schema are you changing exactly?

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: Maybe it is for some reason not part of the etag (even though everything known to the gui should be in there). The etag doenst change. even after hard reload. I am changing a plugin schema, the one you are seeing on screen. i hide the lab_id. it is visible until i do a hard reload.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: Looking at the code, its pretty obvious.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: Sorry that i put you through this and did not realize this earlier. I am pretty sure now that this is a bug.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: I open an issue and fix it.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: I am just confused that this was not raising problems earlier.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: For the current impl, I only based the etag on the config.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: My thinking that this is enough because the nomad-lab version and  git commit is part of that.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text: But in the light of plugins and not everything being a submodules anymore, the artifacts.js can easily change via a plugin change not reflected in the config. This MR is using the artifact.js directly to compute the etag.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text:
That should fix the issue. https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/merge_requests/1714
Hey markus, thank you. this was raising issues and we communicated it, but not directly with you apparently :D.

title: The need of strg+shift+R
text:
I think this is one of the issues the right person needs to see.
i told people to use strg+shift+r 😄
i checked with hampus, we thought that ctrl+shift+r was the solution
Ctrl+shift+r is always a solution 😉. But seriously, I hope the fix above will solve this issue and people can work with your oasis without ctrl+shift+r.
yes a simple reload now does the job
thanks

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: I have been trying to update a dependecy, `MDAnalysis`.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text:
Right now, the dependency in the `pyproject.toml` from `nomad-parser-plugin-atomistic` is set like this:
```
"mdanalysis>=2.8.0,<3.0.0 ; python_full_version >= '3.10'",
"mdanalysis<2.8 ; python_full_version < '3.10'",
```
In `nomad-schema-plugin-simulation-workflow`, it is
```
"mdanalysis>=2.8.0,<3.0.0",
```
So with NOMAD develop is installed with python 3.12 I would expect the `MDAnalysis` version to be `2.8.0`, but the previous version ends up being installed. Could this be caused by the dependency being stated as
```
mdanalysis==2.7.0         # via nomad-parser-plugins-atomistic, nomad-schema-plugin-simulation-workflow
```
in the `requirements-plugins.txt` of `nomad-FAIR`? Or should I use the same dynamic settings depending on the python version in `nomad-schema-plugin-simulation-workflow` as in `nomad-parser-plugin-atomistic`? Is this in your dev distro? if that's the case, the `requirements-plugins.txt` should have no effect there
No, that's on the deployed version https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/develop/gui/
Locally, the versions are resolved correctly. The versions on develop is set here: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-distro/-/blob/main/requirements.txt?ref_type=heads#L156
That means if I want a dependency update to eventually end up accessible to the users, I have to change it there as well?

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: Thats the only place to change it for it to be accessible to users.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: Thank you, I wasn't aware of that.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text:

- once I make a release of my plugin, the dev deployment will be updated accordingly at midnight.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text:
So for the main deployments (develop, staging, test, prod) the plugins will need to be updated manually. 

> So, if I update a plugin dependency, I need to also change the requirements file on nomad-FAIR? We only have the plugins in nomad-FAIR to run the test suite, there's a related issue for this (https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/2255).

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: Once those tests are removed, we can remove the `default_plugins.txt` entirely.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: So the list on nomad-FAIR doesn't affect any deployments.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text:
I think I'm more confused now 🫣 sorry

I'll try to clarify:

1.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text:
I'm only interested in dev, staging, prod deployments, so we can ignore test Oasis

2. I'm talking about a plugin that is already included in the FAIRmat-NFDI/distro-dev with flexible versioning i.e., >=

I am fairly confident that when I make a release of the plugin, the dev deployment updates the version accordingly, and I assume this is based up to staging and prod in the same way

I guess the confusion is how we handle a shared external dependency of both nomad-lab and a plugin? I guess in this case it makes sense that we have to keep nomad-lab updated accordingly.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: But what exactly is this requirements-plugins.txt referring to.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text:
E.g., I don't think nomad-lab relies on MDanalysis anymore, but plugins like simulation workflow schema, which is in the central deployments, do depend on this. Do we still need the dependency in requirements-plugins.txt, or it's enough that the plugin depends on it within it's toml?
2.  `FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-distro-dev` is only used to configure your local development environments, so it has no effect on any deployments. For the versions used in deployments, it only comes from this [pyproject.toml](https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-distro/-/blob/main/pyproject.toml?ref_type=heads) file and the [requirements](https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-distro/-/blob/main/requirements.txt?ref_type=heads) file generated based on the same toml file.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text:
So no other repositories are involved there 😅
@ahmed_ilyas  Thank you for the explanation, and sorry for not acknowledging sooner.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: I think I am starting to understand this better, at least in principle.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text:
What I don't understand still is the following:

In the toml that you linked, it says: `"nomad-parser-plugins-atomistic>=1.0"`

But the requirements file says: `nomad-parser-plugins-atomistic==1.0.2  # via nomad-distribution (pyproject.toml)`

It looks like these were updated a week ago.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: But the atomistic parsers has several versions above this, e.g., v1.0.3 from 3 weeks ago.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: Why is this not updating in the requirements and how can I fix this?

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: The toml gives a valid set of distributions, the `requirements.txt` is generated using the `scripts/generate_python_dependencies.sh` script. But if you want a specific   version of  nomad-parser-plugins-atomistic in the requirements file, you can bump up the version in the toml file and generate the requirements txt file. I don't want a specific version though, I just always want the latest release version. I thought this would already happen with `"nomad-parser-plugins-atomistic>=1.0"` in the toml. The question is why is this stuck at the 1.0.2 release? In the `scripts/generate_python_dependencies.sh` it will only upgrade nomad-lab and nomad-lab-gui, no plugins are upgraded automatically so they will always keep their locked version.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: The intention here was that no other dependency gets automatically updated and individual plugin authors can upgrade their plugins as they see fit and we have a more controlled roll out.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: Otherwise, if random plugins get updated automatically during the nightly run, the pipeline might start failing due to changes in the plugin.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text: Solved!

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
text:
Thanks @ahmed_ilyas 

To summarize my confusion and the solution: 

As Ahmed explains above, the distro-dev .toml controls the versions on the central deployments. The somewhat unclear aspect (for me) is that the `>=` used in the .toml do not imply that a plugin version will be automatically updated upon a new release. In contrast, the versions will be locked once the requirements file is created. The `>=` is used for flexible versioning between interdependent plugins. To change the version, one must increase the version `>= x.x.x` and then recreate the requirements file with the `update_python_dependencies` script followed by a MR. 🤓

title: Unexpected error from elastic search when sorting search results by column
text: I have a local nomad-distro-dev installation (develop branch) with pynxtools (master branch) and my own plugin (search app only).

title: Unexpected error from elastic search when sorting search results by column
text:
One of the columns in this search app is for `search_quantity = f'data.___file_time#pynxtools.nomad.schema.Root'`, it shows values that I expect to see. I have multiple files uploaded with different `___file_time`, as well as some without this field; they do not fit in the default 20 results shown, so there is a button "load more" at the bottom of the list. If I sort by `___file_time` from old to new (by clicking on the header in gui), "load more" works. If I sort by the same field from new to old, "load more" does nothing, and I see the following message in the terminal:
```
WARNING  elasticsearch        [REDACTED_PHONE]-14T07:57:11 POST http://localhost:9200/nomad_entries_v1/_search [status:400 request:0.005s]
  - nomad.commit: 
  - nomad.deployment: oasis
  - nomad.service: app
  - nomad.version: 1.3.16.dev192+gdc9b8bb2e.d20250414
  - taskName: starlette.middleware.base.BaseHTTPMiddleware.__call__.<locals>.call_next.<locals>.coro
INFO:     127.0.0.1:52644 - "POST /fairdi/nomad/latest/api/v1/entries/query HTTP/1.1" 400 Bad Request
```
Any idea on what might be causing this issue?

title: Unexpected error from elastic search when sorting search results by column
text: Interestingly, it seems that for fields that do not use schema (such as `'mainfile'` or `'entry_id'`) "load more" works regardless of the sorting order. Or it may be related not to the schema but to the fact that all entries have `mainfile` and `entry_id`, while not all of them have `___file_time`.

If you're developing plugins, we recommend using `nomad-distro-dev` as described here: 

https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/docs/howto/develop/setup.html#nomad-distro-dev-development-environment-for-the-core-nomad-package-and-nomad-plugins
@Trogg: The documentation page you pointed to (https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/docs/howto/develop/setup.html#installation) is actually quite outdated, and I would not recommend following that procedure anymore. We now instead recommend using what we call a *distribution*:  a single GitHub repository that configures your Oasis setup including the core `nomad-lab` package version, plugins versions, `docker-compose.yaml` and `nomad.yaml`. There are two flavours of these distributions:
 - For production setups: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-distro-template
 - For development setups: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-distro-dev

Depending on your use case, I would pick one of those and follow the README file to setup everything.

title: Unexpected error in the research app
text:
Maybe you could clarify a few points:
 - You mentioned that you are here using `nomad-distro-dev`, is this correct?
 - You also mention that "We are installing the nomad distribution from a server of our institution and than trying to access from the local browser of  our PC.". Do I understand correctly that you run `nomad-distro-dev` on some institution server, and then try to access it from another computer over the network? This sounds like a use case for which `nomad-distro-dev` is not primarily designed for: you would have to be quite careful in opening the correct ports etc. `nomad-distro-dev` is primarily designed all to be run from a single computer and accessed only from that same computer as it does not have a proxy that could redirect the traffic correctly.

title: Unexpected error in the research app
text: With these clarifications we might be able to help you further.

title: Unwanted warning for NumberEditQuantity of unit='dimensionless'
text:
I have a `NumberEditQuantity` defined as follows: 
```py
common_beam_mask = Quantity(
        type=int,
        description='common beam mask',
        a_eln={
            'component': 'NumberEditQuantity',
            'minValue': 0,
            'maxValue': 100,
        },
        unit='dimensionless',
    )
```
In the GUI, when I click and select the "Unit" div, and then un-select by clicking elsewhere, a warning in red pops up "Unit is required" (attaching a screencast).

title: Unwanted warning for NumberEditQuantity of unit='dimensionless'
text: The warning is not desirable in this case.

title: Unwanted warning for NumberEditQuantity of unit='dimensionless'
text:
But dimensionless is a unit. What happens when you click the downarrow?
there's nothing in the dropdown
I think we can remove the unit div altogether in case unit=dimensionless, just like we have it when no unit is specified
or fill the "unit" div with "dimensionless" 😄
yeah, or add some "-" symbol
(still the quantity has units, so for some stuff you might wanna do `common_bean_mask.magnitude`)
yeah, exactly
Does is work if you simply ommit the unit in the schema? It works fine in that case, however, the quantity is not of type `pint.Quantity` in that case
I would just remove it for now
That's what we have everywhere else
I think dimensionless still implies some sort of quantity
And here you just have an ID or what is this?

title: Unwanted warning for NumberEditQuantity of unit='dimensionless'
text: But definitely something that should be fixed long term.

title: Unwanted warning for NumberEditQuantity of unit='dimensionless'
text:
Maybe write an issue in Gitlab
A better quantity description might also be needed as you can tell 😉
Or is it the fraction of the beam that is blocked?

title: Unwanted warning for NumberEditQuantity of unit='dimensionless'
text: Yeah, if it was a mere ID, having an int type makes total sense.

title: Unwanted warning for NumberEditQuantity of unit='dimensionless'
text:
But this is a value coming from the instrument and ideally should be treated as a quantity for consistency with other quantities
I am not 100 percent sure, but i think its a fraction
I think we should probably have a unit for attenuation
there are other quantities like gain values which have the same issue
Okay, just write an issue on Gitlab for the gui problem I guess and the unit itself we can discuss next week when I'm back
alright!! 😄

title: Updating distro-dev after pydantic update
text: After the pydantic update, I updated my nomad-FAIR branch to the head of develop, then was getting some pydantic error in the `EntryPoint()` class if I tried to run a test. I noticed that the local environment still had pydantic 1 installed, and I thought I might have to update the forked distro, so I followed the instructions for that, but then was getting `error: No interpreter found for Python >=3.12.6, <3.13 in managed installations or system path` whenever I run uv. Long story short, I started from scratch cloning my fork, merging the upstream distro-dev to my branch, and then performing the setup as usual, but I still get the above interpreter error.

title: Updating requirements breaks CI/CD
text: I have a MR in nomad-FAIR (https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/merge_requests/2150) that updates the `default_plugins` with a new version of `pynxtools`.

title: Updating requirements breaks CI/CD
text: In order to bring these changes in, I reran `scripts/generate_python_dependencies.sh`.

title: Updating requirements breaks CI/CD
text: The newly generated `requirements*` lead to a failure in the CI/CD. I am not 100% sure but I think this is due to updating to `httpx==0.28.0`, which leads to a failure in the `starlette` package (see [here](https://github.com/encode/starlette/issues/2781) for a similar issue in starlette).

title: Updating requirements breaks CI/CD
text: If we require `httpx<0.27`, it works again.

title: Updating requirements breaks CI/CD
text: I think the issue is fixed in newer versions of `starlette`, but we can't update it because FastAPI is fixed to `<0.100`.

title: Updating requirements breaks CI/CD
text: Any suggestions?

title: Updating requirements breaks CI/CD
text: Can we just limit the `httpx`version?

title: Updating requirements breaks CI/CD
text:
I had a branch for this but auto merge seemingly failed there, can you rebase and see if that fixes it? https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/merge_requests/2237
Great, I'll have a try.

title: Updating requirements breaks CI/CD
text:
Thanks for the fast reply
That worked, thanks

text: Hey Nomad, I have an issue with uploading from my local oasis to central NOMAD.

title: Uploading from Oasis to central NOMAD
text: I clicked on the button upload to central nomad, but then I got the following error: "Processing failed KeycloakAuthenticationError: 401: b'{"error":"invalid_grant","error_description":"Invalid user credentials"}', 5/5 entries processed".

title: Uploading from Oasis to central NOMAD
text: What do I do wrong?

title: Uploading from Oasis to central NOMAD
text: Hi @David_Holec!

title: Uploading from Oasis to central NOMAD
text: The error is related to authentication.

title: Uploading from Oasis to central NOMAD
text:
My first guess would be that the user you logged into with on the Oasis does not exist on our central installation. Could this be the case? Otherwise the issue could very well also be on our side since data migration to the central installation is a fairly experimental feature at the moment.
@David_Holec: After discussing this internally, we realize that there are indeed problems with our current implementation that need to be sorted out. Could I ask more about your use case for data transfer to the central service:
 - Are you mostly interested in this to make your work public and get DOIs and public URLs for people to visit?
 - What kind of data are we talking about: is this DFT calculations or something completely different?
 - Would you be fine if all of the data would be re-processed once transferred to the central deployment?

title: Uploading from Oasis to central NOMAD
text:
Dear @Lauri_Himanen,

Sorry for the late reply.

title: Uploading from Oasis to central NOMAD
text: Firstly, I confirm the account also exists on the central installation.

title: Uploading from Oasis to central NOMAD
text:
Regarding your further questions:
* Are you mostly interested in this to make your work public and get DOIs and public URLs for people to visit?

title: Uploading from Oasis to central NOMAD
text: Yes, indeed.

title: Uploading from Oasis to central NOMAD
text:
This is my primary motivation at this time.

* What kind of data are we talking about: is this DFT calculations or something completely different?

title: Uploading from Oasis to central NOMAD
text:

title: Uploading non compressed files with python
text:
The `uploads` POST endpoint docs says the following:

> There are two basic ways to upload files: in the multipart-formdata or streaming the file data in the http body.

title: Uploading non compressed files with python
text: Both are supported.

title: Uploading non compressed files with python
text: Note, however, that the second method only allows the upload of a single file, and that it does not transfer a filename. If a transfer is made using method 2, you can specify the query argument file_name to name it. This needs to be specified when using method 2, unless you are uploading a zip/tar file (for zip/tar files the names don't matter since they are extracted).

title: Uploading non compressed files with python
text:
So I think your problem might be resolved by adding the `file_name` parameter to your URL. Something like: `'uploads?file_name="my_file.nxs"'`

title: Uploading non compressed files with python
text:
How about this:

```python
import os

def upload_to_NOMAD(nomad_url, token, upload_file):
    '''Upload a single file as a new NOMAD upload. Compressed zip/tar files are
    automatically decompressed.
    '''
    with open(upload_file, 'rb') as f:
        try:
            response = requests.post(
                f'{nomad_url}uploads?file_name={os.path.basename(upload_file)}',
                headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}', 'Accept': 'application/json'},
                data=f, timeout=30)
            upload_id = response.json().get('upload_id')
            if upload_id:
                return upload_id

            print('response is missing upload_id: ')
            print(response.json())
            return
        except Exception as e:
            print('something went wrong uploading to NOMAD')
            return
```
Could you test this out a bit and see if  you have any problems with it? The only difference to your version is that I don't take the whole `upload_file` as `file_name`, as it may be a file path containing folders etc, but only take the "basename". If you are OK with this, I will push it to the docs 👍
@Julien@KAUST: I now  updated our documentation with an improved upload function that also accepts non-compressed files.

title: Uploads are not displayed, despite being in the volumes folder
text: Hi @Aaron_Schüller-Ruhl!

title: Uploads are not displayed, despite being in the volumes folder
text: The docker volumes contain all the information that we store in databases and in our search index. Without this information the raw data and the archives stored in the `.volumes` folder data will not be available. It is still possible to somewhat recover your data by using the raw data. If you don't have thousands of uploads, the simplest method is to move the `fs` folder out of the `.volumes` folder and then re-upload them individually.

title: Uploads are not displayed, despite being in the volumes folder
text: They will then get reprocessed and assigned new identifiers.

title: Uploads are not displayed, despite being in the volumes folder
text: Note that the `staging` folder contains unpublished uploads, and the `public` folder contains published ones.

title: Uploads are not displayed, despite being in the volumes folder
text:
There are also programmatic ways to achieve something similar, so if you wish to recover a lot of data, ask me for further instructions. In the future, if you wish to make a backup of your data so that you are easily able to fully recover it, follow the guide here: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/oasis/admin.html#backups

1. When your upload hangs, it is likely just an "anomali" in processing and often times simply needs to be reprocessed. In this case, you need to request us to do this for you here.

title: Using HDF5
text:
I am going through the documented example for using HDF5 ( https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/develop/docs/howto/customization/hdf5.html ) but am running into the following error

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
TypeError                                 Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[16], line 11
      8 from nomad.datamodel import ArchiveSection
      9 from nomad.datamodel.hdf5 import HDF5Reference
---> 11 class LargeData(ArchiveSection):
     12     value = Quantity(type=HDF5Reference)
     14 upload_files = StagingUploadFiles(upload_id='test_upload', create=True)

Cell In[16], line 12, in LargeData()
     11 class LargeData(ArchiveSection):
---> 12     value = Quantity(type=HDF5Reference)

TypeError: __init__() missing 2 required positional arguments: 'quantity' and 're_pattern'

Help is appreciated
@Alvin_Noe_Ladines can you help here?
you must have imported Quantity from nomad.parsing you must import it from nomad.netainfo
Ahh, thanks! It would be nice if that example was edited to reflect the change in import
Similiarly in the "Write a parser" documentation example there is this code snippet: 

from nomad.datamodel import EntryArchive
from nomad_example.parsers.myparser import MyParser
import logging

p = ExampleParser()
a = EntryArchive()
p.parse('tests/data/example.out', a, logger=logging.getLogger())

print(a.m_to_dict())

but the line p = ExampleParser() should be p = MyParser()
yes thank you, I will make the corrections

title: Using JMESPath syntax in Apps in other widgets than scatter
text:
Hi all,
from the Apps documentation I need to hand over an Axis object to x (and y) of any widget.

title: Using JMESPath syntax in Apps in other widgets than scatter
text:
Doing this I can use JMESPath definitions in the Axis like this:
```python
 MenuItemHistogram(
       x=Axis(
           search_quantity=f'data.preparation.heating[?label_name == `L1`].time#{schema}',
            unit='minute',
        ),
        autorange=True,
        title='LL Heating Time',
        show_input=True,
        n_bins=40,
 ),
```
This works well for scatter plot widgets but it fails with an error that the definition for that search quantity is not found using histograms despite the logic should return a scalar value as it picks one quantity of a defined section out of a repeating section. On the other hand if I just hand over the repeating section without any JMESPath filtering it just seems to take the quantities of all sections.

title: Using JMESPath syntax in Apps in other widgets than scatter
text: Is this a bug or do I oversee something important here?

title: Using `from nomad.client.archive import ArchiveQuery`
text: Hi all, I was trying to figure out a way of querying for a specific entry of mine.

title: Using `from nomad.client.archive import ArchiveQuery`
text:
Here is my initial approach:

```python
query = ArchiveQuery(query={
    "results.material.chemical_formula_descriptive": "C2",
    "metadata.datasets": "my_dataset",
}, 
                     required='*', page_size=2, results_max=100, 
                     url="https://sol-oasis.physik.hu-berlin.de/nomad-oasis/api/v1",
                    username="myname", password="mypassword", owner="user")
```

Unfortunately if gave: `ValueError: Server returns 422: [{'msg': 'metadata.datasets is not a doc quantity', 'loc': [['metadata.datasets']]}]`

So I was wondering if there is either a way of querying for certain datasets OR if I can query upload create time being not older than a certain date.

The error is `pydantic` not finding the package. `cwd` set to NOMAD root works fine, though.

title: VSCode debugger for plugins
text:
This is my `launch.json` setup that fails
```json
        {
            "name": "data test",
            "type": "debugpy",
            "request": "launch",
            "cwd": "${workspaceFolder}/schema/simulation/data/tests",
            "program": "${workspaceFolder}/.pyenv/bin/pytest",

            "env": {

and the one that works

            "program": "${workspaceFolder}/.pyenv/bin/pytest",

                "_PYTEST_RAISE": "1"
            },
            "args": [
                "-sv",
                "--pdb",
                "dependencies/schema/simulation/data/tests",
            ]
        }
```
Does it work if you pip install the plugin instead?

title: Weird GUI data info
text: I saw the sub-sections now are being wrongly capitalized.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: Is this an expected behavior?

title: Weird GUI data info
text: It looks very strange to find things like "Band structure electronic", without the underscores.

title: Weird GUI data info
Weird GUI data info
text: Would that be better?

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
Well, I am thinking on usage: when you look into the metainfo tab, at the end you want to resolve some path `run.calculation.band_structure_electronic`, which with these changes is not clear at all. However, the capitalization and the style of the ELN is not perfect; I will not take it as a reference to change what was there before. ELN serves one purpose, which is prepare and upload data for one part of the users; the data info should serve the purpose of finding the data that you already stored, that's why the ELNs, when pressing the Save button, will follow the archive data style of "non-capitalized" + "underscores"

What I mean with this is: that in the ELN everything is capitalized and clear makes sense, for the data tab, I am not 100% sure of it...

title: Weird GUI data info
text: I agree, what you say.

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
The only problem is, that the ELN is in the data tab/data section so it might not be easy to distinguish the 2 uses
so here in this case, it would look better to have rendering for the subsection names
I disagree. What happens when you press save there? (btw with "Data tab" I meant the metainfo trees we shared, not the section)
At the end this is something that data experts will use, and for them might be easier to see the pythonic underscore and non-capital names.

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
ELNs is just another use, which serve another purpose
@mohammad.nakhaee is being fixed with https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/merge_requests/1693 ?

title: Weird GUI data info
text: It is not intentional.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: The sub sections should show the name of the sub section.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: And those names are lower case.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: It is now either capitalizing the sub section name or (more likely) it is using the section name (e.g. the name of the sub-section "type") instead of the sub sectoin name. I'll say that is confusing, it forces us to keep the same m_def.name for sub-section.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: If I have `k_mesh = SubSection(Mesh)` and `r_mesh = SubSection(Mesh)` are both Mesh? Also, from a data science point of view, what I want to see is `k_mesh` or `r_mesh`, not `K Mesh` nor `R Mesh`.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: Again, ELN serves the purpose of human-filling-blanks.

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
The archived data is a different thing, imho. I think the problem is now, that all subsections (also in Run and Metadata) are rendered with capital letters, but for the quantity it is only applying capitalization and _ removal in the Data section
Removing the "_" might be ok, but the general capitilization of all sub sections and quantities needs to be reverted.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: As @ChemaP  says, if you are somewhat familiar with the underlying schema this rename becomes really really confusing.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: Section names and sub section names are sometimes very similar, often it is just the capitalization that helps to separate them.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: Therefore, to keep them mentally separated the capital or not capital is important.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: We can find solutions to customize specific ELNs, but we cannot apply this to the whole metainfo/archive.

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
I, actually also think the capitalization is not so important for the ELN, but removing the underscores in the ELN will make a big difference for lab scientists. I do think quantity names should have the same rules as subsection names
I agree on the consistency.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: We should then opt to leave everything un-capitalized.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: This will bring them closer to how they are defined in the schema.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: As mentioned many times before, we need the option to add annotations (like label) to the `SubSection()` constructor.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: I think this would solve all this.

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
At the end, NOMAD is a Python schema, so I would go pythonic: all these are attributes of a class called EntryArchive, hence it should follow the typical convention (uncapitalized with underscores as separators)
@Hampus_Näsström But the "label" quantity would be meant for edge cases, not for breaking reasonable conventions. I guess there is tension between staying true to the technicalities of the schema and providing human readable labels. But right now the middle ground is so blurry that it is more confusing than helpful. Yes, but I am fine with keeping the quantity convention as long as it can be overwritten by a label annotation. So, we are reverting the default back to the actual schema name and give you something to overwrite it with a human-readable label.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: Don't we have this already?

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
Or just for quantities? Just for quantities
We don't allow any annotations in the `SubSection()` constructore as far as I can tell
And this is consistently used in editable sections (i.e.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: ELNs) and in read only sections?

title: Weird GUI data info
text: Not sure I follow.

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
Until now the quantity:
 ```py
some_quantity = Quantity(
  ...,
  a_eln=ELNAnnotation(
    component=ELNComponentEnum...
  ),
)
```
has been shown in the gui as "Some quantity"
While:
 ```py
some_quantity = Quantity(
  ...,
)
```
has been shown in the gui as "some_quantity". As we don't have any annotations possible for:
```py
some_sub_section = SubSection(...)
```
it has always been displayed as "some_sub_section"
Ok, apparently the ELN and the non ELN are not consistent. ELNs "humanize" the shown names and the normal archive browser keeps the original technical names.

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
Exactly
Assuming that we actually want this.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: The ELN quantity and the archive quanity are two implementations.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: The sub section has only one. So the recent changes to the sub section fixed it for  ELNs, but broke it for the archive viewer.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: And this is where @ChemaP  opened this post.

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
I'm not sure what we want to be honest. I would like to be able to give annotations like default display unit and label also to non-ediatable quantities (like arrays which we typically don't make editable in an ELN schema). And also to sub sections so that I can for example set the label or include a sub section into the overview page
The wish for one mechanism to apply a custom label and then having this mechanism applied consistently to both the ELN and the archive seem reasonable.

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
However, I see two problems:
1.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: It opens the door for you to have one schema using completely different conventions then the next.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: But I guess this is the downside of any customization.

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
I can live with it.
2.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: You will always find people that do not want to see the humanized labels, but the raw technical keys. Which means we probably need an option for the user to switch between labels and raw keys (or somehow show them both).

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
I think somehow showing them both would be nice but all this might be too much patching for the old GUI. I, personally, would be fine with having the raw keys as default (for both quantities and sub sections) as long as I can always specify a label in the schema if I want to. But that is just my opinion and I'm pretty sure others would disagree 😅
I agree with @Hampus_Näsström , we need to be able to put a label for the sub sections. Ok, when there is the time, I will revert the last change and instead add a possibility to have a custom label on both the quantities and subsections.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: I guess this should also satisfy @ChemaP ?

title: Weird GUI data info
text: That sounds good.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: Thanks!

title: Weird GUI data info
text: I had at the brief look at this in the code.

title: Weird GUI data info
text: This will be a more complex thing. For now I revert the changes that started this and we will have a more detailed discussion and solution once @mohammad.nakhaee  is back.

title: Weird GUI data info
text:
Issue: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/1915
Revert MR: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/merge_requests/1703

title: When using the m_copy method, there are problems with the attributes overwriting
text:
me and @Hampus_Näsström were getting these sort of errors:
```
Error: Units do not match ('mol / minute' != 'meter^3 / second')
```
when defining such a quantity:
```
class VolumetricFlowRate(TimeSeries):
    value = TimeSeries.value.m_copy()
    value.unit = 'meter ** 3 / second'
    set_value = TimeSeries.set_value.m_copy()
    set_value.unit = 'meter ** 3 / second'
```
if we redefine the quantities, this error does not arise.

title: When using the m_copy method, there are problems with the attributes overwriting
text: Thing is that the unit 'mol / minute' is not in a parent class, but in several sibling ones.

title: When using the m_copy method, there are problems with the attributes overwriting
text: This bug report is just to point out the odd behaviour of m_copy.

title: When using the m_copy method, there are problems with the attributes overwriting
text:
We can keep redefining quantities in the meanwhile that this issue is sorted out
Could you create a minimal example with which we can reproduce the problem? You can then post this as a GitLab issue directly.

title: Writing tests for plugin and parsing
text: in our plugins the parsers look for samples bei `results.eln.lab_ids` and links them.

title: Writing tests for plugin and parsing
text:
When writing tests for that parsers i get this error:
`nomad.search.QueryValidationError: results.eln.lab_ids is not a doc quantity`  why is it not a doc quantity?
i also get annother validation error: `nomad.search.QueryValidationError: upload_id is not a doc quantity`
So during your test you are doing an API call or using the `nomad.search` module I guess? I don't think that by default any of the search infrastructure is setup for tests, as typically you only work with the data contained in the archive itself. That is probably why you are getting that error. But I could probably say more if I see the test code that you are trying to run. I think the safest and fastest choice for tests is to mock some of the API/search calls. You can learn more about mocking with pytest here: https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/how-to/monkeypatch.html

The other alternative is that in your test pipelines you really do setup the whole required infrastructure, but that might get tricky.
my parsers use the `nomad.search` module, the test it self is basically just matching a file to the parser
i will try with the mocks 🙂 thanks
that worked thanks

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text: So I can at least reproduce this.

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text: The quick fix for now, put a `required='*'` into your calls, e.g. `ArchiveQuery(required='*')` gives you results. For some reason the default value for required is `{'run': '*'}`. The query is automatically narrowed to entries that can fulfil the required. If you have no calculations on the Oasis, you can `0` results. The `required={}` is wrong btw, because this mean you don't want to require anything, `require='*'` means to require everything.

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text: I have a fix now.

Hi @Theodore_Chang_DM_BITTE, I am using the following query:
```py
from nomad.client import ArchiveQuery
query = ArchiveQuery(
    url = "https://nomad.support.ikz-berlin.de/nomad-oasis/api/v1",
    query = {},
    required= {'*'},
)
query.fetch()
res = await query.async_download(1)
```
It fetches the entries (around 200 of them) but throws the type error I shared above when downloading
You are still giving a set to `required` and not a string. It has to be `required='*'` and not `required={'*'}`.

But for entries belonging to certain entry classes from plugins, I run into an AttributeError when accessing `context.name`. The error is attached along. @ChemaP ran into the similar error and it seems it was resolved here https://discord.com/channels/[REDACTED_PHONE]/[REDACTED_PHONE]

The error can be reproduced on nomad test oasis by running the following code in the jupyterhub:
```py

    url = "https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis",
    query = {
        'results.eln.sections:any':['ELNXRayDiffraction'],
    },
    required= '*',
)
res = await query.async_download(2)
```
@Sebastian_Brückner @Theodore_Chang_DM_BITTE @Markus_Scheidgen Is someone looking into this?

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text: This thing uses `ELNXRayDiffraction` which points to `nomad_measurements.xrd.schema.ELNXRayDiffraction`, if the environment has no such a schema, then it cannot be resolved. It will not work if the necessary definitions cannot be found.

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text: Hmm.

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text: But the schema is available as a 'built-in schema' when creating an entry.

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text: Shouldn't that indicate that the environment has it defined?

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text:
Are we missing in the nomad configs of the plugin? Here's the link to all nomad yamls we have related to XRayDiffraction

https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-measurements/blob/main/nomad.yaml

https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-measurements/blob/main/src/nomad_measurements/nomad_plugin.yaml

https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-measurements/blob/main/src/nomad_measurements/xrd/nomad_plugin.yaml

https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-measurements/blob/main/src/nomad_measurements/xrd/parser/nomad_plugin.yaml
I do not know how those are loaded.

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text: You simply try to import the package before the download call. If the package cannot be imported then there is no way to find the schemas, you need to make sure those definitions can be located.

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text: Keep in mind there are **two** environents at play.

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text: One is on the server, the other is on the client side. At the moment there is no schema transfer to the client. So also the client needs to have the schema available to instantiate it with the retrieved data.

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text: The default jupyter lab north tool does not have any plugins installed.

title: `ArchiveQuery` fetches nothing in Oasis
text:
For now, you will need to manually pip install the necessary plugins at the moment. There are several ways how we could mitigate this in the future:
- put official FAIRmat plugins into the jupyter lab build
- put out a distribution python package with nomad-lab + the offficial FAIRmat plugins
- allows to transfer schemas with the archive query (this could also be a fall back to other solutions, but you will loose any normalize functions in the transfer)

If you cannot wait, you can:
- put a `!pip install ...` cell into your notebooks
- have your own jupyter lab image with plugins for the IKZ oasis
Ah I see, it makes a lot of sense. I installed the plugin in the Jupyter env and ArchiveQuery works like a charm! However installing it this way isn't convenient because if the plugin is not pip installable, one needs to pull the plugin repo inside Jupyter and install it locally.
"allows to transfer schemas with the archive query (this could also be a fall back to other solutions, but you will loose any normalize functions in the transfer)"

This is a nice solution, as it reduces the requirements on the client side. At least, the user can get the structured data object, allowing it to be an alternative to  `requests` + `parse`.  installing the plugin only becomes necessary when normalize functions are to be used

title: displaying non-symmetric tensors in GUI
text:
Hi all,

does the following code mean that our matrix renderer always swaps rows/cols:

`# gui/src/components/archive/visualizations.js

  let value = shape.length > 1 ? ({rowIndex, columnIndex}) => values[columnIndex][rowIndex] : ({columnIndex}) => values[columnIndex]
  if (invert) {
    value = shape.length > 1 ? ({rowIndex, columnIndex}) => values[rowIndex][columnIndex] : ({rowIndex}) => values[rowIndex]
  }`

`invert` is only applied to 1‑D vectors, so every 2‑D tensor (e.g. 3×3 NMR, stress, dielectric) is displayed transposed compared to the stored archive data.

title: displaying non-symmetric tensors in GUI
text:
To sum up: **why does the table widget transpose the tensor?**

Please also find attached a screenshot from a user
@Lauri_Himanen or @mohammad.nakhaee could help here
In the case of a 1D array, there is no change in how the actual value is accessed. However, for 2D arrays, setting invert effectively transposes the matrix by swapping rowIndex and columnIndex when accessing values. invert only affects how the data is visualized, not the actual stored data. The decision to apply invert is determined by the schema. different theories and methods may represent tensors differently
@mohammad.nakhaee thank you very much! What would be then the best practice for the use cases, in which we wish to skip transposing the tensors in the GUI, because we need exactly the tensor in the archive and showing a `Matrix.T` would be scientifically false?
e.g. :

if we let the parser transpose the data as `Matrix.T`, GUI displays the data correctly ✅ , but the stored data in the archive is now transposed ⛔ 

if we let the parser parse the data as `Matrix`, the stored data is fine ✅ , but GUI implies otherwise ⛔
In general I'm really not sure why the UI should transpose the data: I guess it is a matter of how one is used to seeing the data displayed and stored (row-major order and column-major order). and the original person who implemented the visualization (Markus?) made a choice. I have zero objections in getting rid of the inversion directly in Javascript.

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: Would it somehow be possible to decide if a widget appears in an app depending if it is populated by any data points given the current  set of filters?

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: So e.g. to make empty widgets invisible?

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: Maybe @Lauri_Himanen  has an idea if this could be possible?

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: A possible alternative (more straight forward) would be to have parallel apps with more locked filters?

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: I was planning this for the solar cell ones.

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: Then, I would day that the app navigation menu would need some update...

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: Since the dashboard is designed to have a specific layout, we probably should not simply remove empty widgets.

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text:
I think the better alternative is either parallel apps, or parallel dashboards.
parallel dashboards like in here?

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text:
This could make sense.
this looks really good.

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text:
Is this already part of a MR? @Pepe_Marquez
this is actually a screenshot of the legacy NOMAD 😆
should have know, the color😅
https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/rae/gui/search
how difficult is it to adapt the app with parallel dashboards?

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: Not very hard.

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: Could you give an example of the use case that you have in mind?

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text:
I would just want to understand the use case better to see if a separate app or parallel dashboards makes more sense.
the current catalysis app on the oasis has a lot of widgets on one page. It would be great if we could make it less overwhelming for the user by allowing them to choose the relevant widgets, by e.g. grouping them.

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: The widgets at the moment have several "themes" from top to bottom: reaction (name, reactant, product), catalyst properties (elemental comp., preparation method, catalyst type), reaction conditions (temp., gas concentrations, pressure, whsv), reaction results (scatterplots with T vs.

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: Conversion, T vs. Selectivity, 2 plots with Conversion of a specific example reactant vs. Selectivity of a specific product).

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: Especially  one of the last 2 plots will be empty if some filter has been set at the top.

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text:
I think with either parallel dashboards we can keep the whole page less overwhelming or maybe with some "toggle/hide" buttons for a group of widgets, so that they only appear if the users chooses so see them. But the filters will still be relevant from one "group" of widgets to the next, so I dont think parallel apps is what we need in this case? Let me know if I can explain better in a meeting @Lauri_Himanen
I think I get the idea.

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: How would you prefer the switching between the different dashborads to happen, and would swithincg the dashboard affect anything else, e.g. would it toggle on/off some filters?

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: Would you @Pepe_Marquez also use this feature if it was available?

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text:
I would want to get an idea of how  relevant this feature is for users.
switching the dashboards I would want to leave the applied filters on as selected. I wrote this basic issue with some ideas a long time ago that I wanted to get done (I did not manage to have the time yet). Perhaps this gives you a better idea? https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-perovskite-solar-cells-database/issues/18
I think if easier, I could possible live with the same left filters and columns... I'm just worried that we end up with a solution that has too narrow of a scope. Soon someone will want that when switching the dashboard, also the columns, filters and menus change, and we will be back to the idea of just writing separate Apps. Another possibility is to be able to group apps together, and then have an easier way to switch between the apps in a group.. This way Julia could e.g. have separate apps for reactions, catalyst properties etc., amd the interface could just have some easy way to switch between these. I think this would be the easiest. If someone would want to re-use the same columns and filters, they can just import them or copy them. Can you copy a set of filter settings from one app to the next in the gui? Do you mean that can the active query be copied, and "pasted" into another app?
yes, that was what I meant. That would be the difference between one app with several dashboards or several (grouped) apps, right? in the former there is a common active query, whereas in the latter case I would have to set the active query again.

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text: Do I understand this right?

title: dynamic appearance of app widgets
text:
You are right that the active query does not transfer between apps automatically, and there is no yet anything for copy/pasting the query. So if we use multiple apps, we gain complete control over all aspects, but probably cannot very easily communicate information such as the active queries when switching between the apps.
