title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Hi all, I have a student working on a mini thesis where we want to try doing some machine learning from general NOMAD data, and in practice most of the work is figuring how we should filter the results to get only data with reasonable methodology and precision so that we are not training on complete crap...

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: The results precision parameters, like k_line_density, planewave cutoff, etc. are quite helpful for us.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Obviously the parser support is not always great, but I think we could help there a bit at least for the cases we need.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: However what I noticed is that specifically the `k_line_density` parameter is sometimes wrong.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: I think there are at least two issues.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text:
The parameter itself is the minimum density calculated from x,y,z k-points `k_mesh.grid` and reciprocal cell vectors as expected. The issues seems to be that when parser doesn't provide the `k_mesh.grid` but only the list of k-points. In that case the specific logic to construct the `k_mesh.grid` from k-points doesn't IMO work in two specific cases:

https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/normalizing/method.py?ref_type=heads#L367
and lines below have the exact logic...
1 - symmetry is completely ignored, parsers output only the irreducible list of k-point into  `k_mesh.points`. For example OpenMX doesn't set method.k_mesh, but in eigenvalues sets the  `k_mesh.points` (the values are actually printed only for irreducible k-points).

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: We don't set either multiplicities nor weights (because the code doesn't print them), but I can add them.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: OpenMX only uses the inversion symmetry, so multiplicity is 1 for Gamma and 2 for anything else (at least for non-collinear non-spinorbit).

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text:
Therefore in practice https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/normalizing/method.py?ref_type=heads#L401 `k_mesh.grid = [len(set(k_mesh.points[:, i])) for i in range(3)]` will for example end with 8 8 4 grid, while the original grid was 8 8 8 (and it was reduced with inversion symmetry). Thus we have half the k_line_density we should have 

2 - Obviously the k-mesh will be completely wrong for non-uniform grids, but hopefully this should be fringe enough to not be a big concern, but another check that the k-points are uniformly spaced could be not that difficult?

3 - if the k_mesh.grid is provided but not the points, the points will be reconstructed (at least for Gamma-centered mesh) https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/normalizing/method.py?ref_type=heads#L378  `points = np.meshgrid(*[np.linspace(0, 1, n) for n in k_mesh.grid])`. This however will create the full grid, so again will be inconsistent with the parsers which provide the symmetry reduced ones into `points` , so IMO we should save this one into `all_points` and duplicate them into points only in case of P1 symmetry.

4 - https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/normalizing/method.py?ref_type=heads#L370 k_mesh.dimensionality will be 3 for all of  1x1x1, 1x1x6, and 20x20x1 grids... is this expected and it is the way the metainfo definition is intended? What I think we should do (we can take a stab at some of this, but at this point I'm looking for a agreement on prefered way to move forward here):
Only construct the `k_mesh.grid` if either `all_points`  or `points` and one of  `multiplicities` or `weights` are provided (or points are provided and all mupltiplicities are one).

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: In the second case we could use the normalized reciprocal cell, get list of symmetry operations and extend the points to all_points, while double checking that the weights/multiplicities match. And only from `all_points` we could try guessing the grid (preferably after also checking the uniform spacings).

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: If multiplicities/weights are not provided, we just abort.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: IMO its better to provide no `k_line_density` value than a wrong value.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text:
Also some questions about my uderstanding of the metainfo in the end.
- What is actually the difference between `k_mesh.weights` and `k_mesh.multiplicities`. 
- And just to be sure about the metainfo definitions: `n_points == len(all_points) == sum(multiplicities)` right? CC @Nathan_Daelman @Nathan_Daelman (sorry for double CC, wasn't sure which one is the correct one)  as the author of the original code in the normalizer and looking for feedback.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Hi Pavel, thank you for picking this particular issue apart so well!

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: The `k_line_density` was originally envisioned to be quite restraint, but I agree that either (a) its limits should be extended or (b) better demarcated.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: The former is obviously nicer, but hinges heavily on adding k-space symmetry analysis, as you indicated.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: I have to see with @Joseph_Rudzinski when I take this.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Or maybe it gets delegated to somebody else?

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Regardless, thank you for tagging me.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text:
We'll get back to you early next week, is that fine. 👋
Yeah, this would be much appreciated, but as I said, for the things I need I can send patches, I just wanted to check beforehand about the big picture. In fact @ChemaP already pointed out that https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-simulations could be the the way forward and I'll be meeting him Monday 10AM to discuss, so what I'm looking for is more  of a feedback or general consensus how to fix this (in a way that is acceptable upstream).

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Yes, we talk on Monday.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text:
Sorry, it was easy for me to overlook things today, as I am on day off and I was outside 😅
@Pavel_Ondračka it seems that this issue has landed with me. Most important for me is to know **your time frame**, as I'll be going on holidays for 2 weeks next Monday.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Regarding the specifics, are you most interested in OpenMX data or also that from other code?

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: I'm asking since the fix may involve both the normalizer and the parser.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text:
Going over each of your points:
1.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text:
There are actually 2 issues here:
   1.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: One is where we read the k-points from the schema.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Our new schema will always have a single storage place and *reference* it where necessary.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text:
In the old schema, it might be more interesting to give the parser control over which place to read from.
   2.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: The other is *symmetry*. I think that unfolding the grid goes beyond the scope of a fix (though we may consider it as a feature in the future). I would see to leverage the *multiplicities* here.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text:
You mentioned that for OpenMX these would trivial to set (all 1 I assume)?
2.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Fully agreed.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text:
I'll see to add this check.
3.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text:
If we fix point 1, wouldn't that also resolve this issue?
4.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Very astute observation!

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: I'd say it depends: if a 2D system is modeled in 3D, the extra dimension should not count. If it is truly 3D, then the dimension with only 1 k-point may indeed be the precision bottleneck, and `k_line_density` should point that out.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Typically codes are pretty clear on a system's dimensionality, but some (e.g.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: VASP) always defaults to 3D, or the user does not leverage the dimension control well...

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Luckily we have a package that ascertains the dimensionality independently: MatID by @Lauri_Himanen Now it just becomes a question of the normalization order: `MethodNormalizer` has to come after.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text:
We might also call the analyzer 2x, but it's a pretty expensive routine.
@Pavel_Ondračka I've been working on supporting your feedback, especially the symmetry-reduced grid.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: I don't think that we can cover all cases with multiplicities (unless we unfold the symmetrized grid).

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Definitely simple symmetries like cubic and orthorhombic are feasible within a short time frame.

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text: Do you know, or could you point me to _which brillouin zones are symmetry-reduced in OpenMX_?

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
text:
In general, many codes only support the simpler cases
I also have a hunch of how to extend the _k-line density_ to something like a _k-density_.

text:
Actually, the API should have provided the GUI with the entry archive having the references on the first place as entry_references. See it on oasis-b:
In the new version, no such list is provided in metadata
So something got wrong with the normalizers which should have filled this part automatically... Actually, it is EntryMetadata's collect_references which should do the job, but https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/datamodel/datamodel.py?ref_type=heads&blame=0#L1037 returns a dictionary with the references in it and neither an MProxy nor a list as expected below.
the dict then contains the proper reference and not an MPRoxy
so the final fix: 
            if isinstance(current_value, dict):
                current_value= [ref.value for ref in current_value.values()]
Although it may should sit inside MSection.m_get... The first MetainfoReferenceError is caused by the wrong module name, which is used to resolve the definition. The second issue is about collecting references, so the function does not account for references using full storage. If this is about the History card, then it should only be picking Activities by design.

title: non-editable Quantities definition
text:
Hi NOMAD team,
To have a non editable Quantity in NOMAD there are different ways.

title: non-editable Quantities definition
text:
One way is to leave out the a_eln part at Quantity definition like:
```python
lab_id = Quantity(
        type=str,
        description="""An ID string that is unique at least for the lab that produced this
            data."""
    )
```
But it appears a bit ugly in the GUI and might be the choise if there is a need for some indexing needed by code or a query and not for to be looked up by the user. Another way from the documentation should be:
```python
name = Quantity(
        type=str,
        description='A short human readable and descriptive name.',
        a_eln=dict(component='StringEditQuantity', label='name'),
        a_display=dict(editable=False)
    )
```
But that seems not to work.

title: non-editable Quantities definition
text: If I try it I am still able to edit.

title: non-editable Quantities definition
non-editable Quantities definition
text: Can someone tell me why approach #2 is not working and what is the recommended way to do it?

title: non-editable Quantities definition
text: From the docs that should indeed work. @mohammad.nakhaee any idea why it doesn't?

title: non-editable Quantities definition
text:
Hi @Matthias_Zelt 

You should be able to use something like:

```python

name = Quantity(
        type=str,
        description='A short human readable and descriptive name.',
        a_eln=dict(component='StringEditQuantity', label='name'),
        a_display=dict(editable={'exclude': 'name'})
    )

```

or more properly:

```python
name = Quantity(
        type=str,
        description='A short human readable and descriptive name.',
        a_eln=dict(component='StringEditQuantity', label='name'),
        a_display=DisplayAnnotation(Filter(exclude='name'))
    )
```
sorry, I meant the `a_display` should be added to the section definition where the quantity lives
Ok.

title: non-editable Quantities definition
text: What you stated above does not work.

title: non-editable Quantities definition
text:
Adding it to the section is basically what I wrote above (3rd option) isn't it? However, documentation still says it can also be defined with the Quantity
DisplayAnnotation is intended for the Section level, not for Quantity. It is as a filter of quantity names.

title: non-editable Quantities definition
text: Ok, thanks.

title: non-editable Quantities definition
text:
But then you should change the doc:
OK I was not aware of this so it should be a bug if it doesnt work

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
text:
Hello,
I have an entry with a quantity that can also contain `np.nan` (`dtype float64`).

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
text: When accessing this entry via API with requests everything works as expected.

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
text: But when using the ArchiveQuery (`from nomad.client.archive import ArchiveQuery`) the `np.nan` becomes a problem. Because the error `TypeError: Cannot cast array data from dtype('O') to dtype('float64') according to the rule 'safe' ` does not let me download the entry.

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
text: I was told that a related np.nan problem has been discussed here (https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/2128).

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
np.nan and None in nomad client
text: And the typing system does not allow this beahviour due to data safety.

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
text: What you can do: turn on `as_plain_dict=True` so that download as plain dicts. Work with plain dicts if possible.

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
text: If you need EntryArchives, you need to manually replace affected nones with either nan or inf.

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
text: Then call `m_from_dict`, reference code can be seen as attached.

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
text: Ok, thank you for the input.

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
text: I want to resolve references from my entry so the EntryArchive would be nice.

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
text: I will give it a try with `m_from_dict`.

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
text: I forget to mention that, if you have write access to the source code, you can modify this block of code in ArchiveQuery.

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
text:
There is a hidden flag "treat_none_as_nan", so you can simply change line 476 into:

```py
result = EntryArchive.m_from_dict(
    response_json[index]['archive'], m_context=context, treat_none_as_nan=True
)
```
Thank you very much. `treat_none_as_nan` works great for now.

title: publishing data from a NOMAD-oasis
text:
Dear developers,

I have some questions regarding publishing data from a NOMAD-oasis.

title: publishing data from a NOMAD-oasis
text:
We have:

    a local NOMAD-oasis installation
    different simulation and measurement data-set for magnetic materials which are read via our own developed parsers (many different file formats like txt, json, hdf5 (nexus, non-nexus), ...)


We need: 

    to publish our datasets on central NOMAD.

title: publishing data from a NOMAD-oasis
text:
Is it possible to publish on the oasis without moving data to the central one?
    possibility to test the publishing process. How is this currently possible (Publish it on local NOMAD than publishing on/to central NOMAD)? The NOMAD-documentation only states (https://nomad-lab.eu/nomad-lab/nomad-oasis.html):
"Publish selected data: NOMAD Oasis will soon be connected to the public NOMAD service, allowing you to publish selected data with one click."

Can one please point me into the right direction.

title: publishing data from a NOMAD-oasis
text: I/we are also open for online meetings.

title: publishing data from a NOMAD-oasis
text:
Thanks in advance,
BR
Wilfried
Crosslink to a related discussion: "Publishing an entry from Oasis to Central NOMAD" https://discord.com/channels/[REDACTED_PHONE]/[REDACTED_PHONE]
Hi @Wilfried_Hortschitz, We are having a bit of a intense time on our side due to the preparation of some important meetings and deadlines coming on our side.

title: publishing data from a NOMAD-oasis
text: Sorry if our responses come late.

title: publishing data from a NOMAD-oasis
text: At the moment, the feature of shipping data from an Oasis with custom plugins to the central NOMAD is not fully covered, meaning that the schemas will not be recognized as those plugins might not be installed in our central sevrice. It is in our agenda to cover this, but we are not fully there. We have to have in mind that there will not be an immediate solution as this can get really hard and is nothing that we can get done in the next few weeks. But it really is in our agenda! I believe we have our first cases of publishing comp data from an Oasis to the central NOMAD, but in this case, both installations were sharing the same plugin relevant for that data.

title: publishing data from a NOMAD-oasis
text: Perhaps @Lauri_Himanen will be able to comment more about the progress. Regarding the question about to publish in an Oasis.

title: publishing data from a NOMAD-oasis
text: Yes, you can do that (on your own risks and rules), but to generate a DOI in those datasets you will need to configure your NOMAD installation to have a DOI provider (https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/docs/reference/config.html#datacite). Also, I guess you will need to have your Oasis openly available (not only on your intranet).

title: publishing data from a NOMAD-oasis
text: I am sorry if the answers from the relevant experts these days do not come super fast.

title: publishing data from a NOMAD-oasis
text: Busy days! 🙏

title: pynxtools with config files - wrong way of passing the config file?
text: Hi guys, I am using pynxtools with some personalized config file to parse some UPS data (vms file) and metadata (eln) into a nexus file. Now it works in some environments and some not.

title: pynxtools with config files - wrong way of passing the config file?
text:
It seems to me that I have the same version of pynxtools and almost the same version of pynxtools-xps in both environment. More precisely, I have pynxtools 0.7.4 in both and pynxtools-xps 0.4.6 in the one that doesn't work and 0.4.6.post1.dev2+g81d414d.d20241117 in the one that works. The precise command I am using is `convert([str(Data_file), str(Setup_ELN)], "xps", "NXmpes", Nexus_file_name, config_file = r'\mypath\config_vms.json')` 

If I execute that with the environment that has pynxtools-xps 0.4.6 , it seems to not recognize the config_file option: File "C:\Users\gorenfjf\Anaconda3\lib\site-packages\pynxtools_xps\reader_utils.py", line 89, in parse_file
    `self.raw_data = self.parser.parse_file(file, **kwargs)
TypeError: parse_file()   got an unexpected keyword argument 'config_file'`

Sorry, maybe It's not a problem of pynxtools version, it's just my first guess.

Depending on the reader, you can also pass the config file as part of the "files" argument. That's what I am doing with the mpes reader. For the xps reader, @Lukas_Pielsticker should know. For the context: I am trying to dock my app.

title: pynxtools with config files - wrong way of passing the config file?
text: But my environment has so much history of things being installed/re-installed from here and there, that it's really difficult to figure out what is needed and what is not.

title: pynxtools with config files - wrong way of passing the config file?
text:

The config_file option was introduced with the multi_format_reader, and the corresponding changes to the XPS reader.

text:

text:

Is there any chance that some update have been introduced without changing the version number? Like I tried to put the same version of pynxtools - based on its name -in my new environment, but it is actually not the same? No, but I guess I just misunderstand the error message. As you said the config_file should be a possible argument ... unless the syntax has changed :/
I can maybe look into this tonight
I'm just afraid I modified something else than the config file in my local copy 😅  But again, I get the bug even when I *pip install pynxtools-xps* from my local copy. So that should not be the problem :/
thank you! I must admit I don''t know the xps reader well enough to help with this. Could you post the package versions you are using in the two environments, i.e. the output of pip list | grep pynxtools
Anyways, passing the config file as part of the input_files should work

title: questions about nomad-distro-dev
text:
Next question, the `uv add packages/nomad-parser-plugins-electronic` command does not work. I have added multiple packages, and now when I do the `uv add ...` it always complains about some other packages missing in `tool.uv.sources` do you have to immediately run the uv add command after running each single `git submodule add ...`?
``` 
uv add packages/nomad-parser-plugins-electronic/
  × Failed to build `nomad-measurements @ file:///home/jschumann/nomad-distro-dev/packages/nomad-measurements`
  ├─▶ Failed to parse entry: `nomad-material-processing`
  ╰─▶ `nomad-material-processing` is included as a workspace member, but is missing an entry in `tool.uv.sources` (e.g.,
      `nomad-material-processing = { workspace = true }`)
```
This is the same error message for the problem that I was discussing today in the cafe. Though I am still trying to find why/when this happens
after I add the other packages manually to the pyproject.toml, the command `uv add ...` works for the last missing package. maybe it just cannot handle adding multiple missing packages at once
Ah yes, it doesn't support adding multiple submodules at the same time followed by `uv add`. If you're using `uv add`, you'll have to run it after adding each submodule individually.

text: Ok thanks, maybe this is also my issue.

title: questions about nomad-distro-dev

I'll have another look
So it works if you've added multiple submodules and list all of the plugins in one command. 

`uv add packages/nomad-measurements packages/PLUGIN_B packages/PLUGIN_C`
Thanks Ahmed!

title: search widget showing more then 5 hits?
text: Unfortunately, the custom filter menu behaves differntly to the search widget and does not show suggestions when start typing in a string.

title: search widget showing more then 5 hits?
text:
Does onyone know? 
@Lauri_Himanen ?

title: search widget showing more then 5 hits?
text:
Suggestions are not available for custom quantities, but are collected for the shared quantities like entry_name, if so specified in their setup. So:
Lab ID is a custom quantity => no suggestions
`entry_name` is one of the quantities shared across schemas, and it has been configured to collect suggestions => suggestions are shown
Thanks. In the search widgets the suggestions work, but not in the filter menu. I also tested for `entry_name` like 
```
MenuItemTerms(
                    search_quantity='entry_name',
                    width=12,
                ),
```
Should the suggestions work in the Filter Menu? There was an issue with the suggestions that was fixed recently in `develop`. The fix is also available in 1.3.14 which is released today.

title: testing workflows
text: I am currently working on the NEB workflow plugin(https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-neb-workflows) and I am not sure how to test if my scripts are working through the terminal. I am currently always spinning up the GUI which does not feel very efficient. For a single computational file I can do `uv run nomad parse neb0.traj > output` but if I try to parse the workflow file directly I get the error `AssertionError: Only archives with upload_id can be referenced` which makes sense. I saw a testing.py script from Chema in the neb repo, which looks like it is possible to resolve the references from the workflow through the terminal, but I am not sure how to use it, or how to do something similar. Looking at the testing_AlCo2S4.py file in [tests](https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-neb-workflows/blob/develop/tests/test_AlCo2S4.py), is there a way to run this and inspect an output for the script?

title: testing workflows
text: Maybe @Alvin_Noe_Ladines has some tips?

title: testing workflows
text:
Hi Julia, you can adap the following script for your testing ```from nomad.datamodel import EntryArchive, EntryMetadata
from nomad.datamodel.context import ServerContext
from nomad.files import StagingUploadFiles
from nomad.processing import Upload
from nomad.parsing.parser import ArchiveParser
from electronicparsers.yambo import YamboParser
import sys
from nomad.utils import get_logger

logger = get_logger(__name__)

upload_files = StagingUploadFiles(upload_id='test_upload', create=True)
upload = Upload(upload_id='test_upload')
context = ServerContext(upload=upload)

yambo_archive = EntryArchive(
    m_context=context,
    metadata=EntryMetadata(upload_id=upload.upload_id, entry_id='yambo_entry'),
)

YamboParser().parse(sys.argv[-1], yambo_archive, logger)

workflow_archive = EntryArchive(
    m_context=context,
    metadata=EntryMetadata(upload_id=upload.upload_id, entry_id='workflow_entry'),
)
ArchiveParser().parse(sys.argv[-2], workflow_archive, logger)```
the worklow archive needs a context in order to resolve the referenced archive, created an upload test_upload containing an entry yambo_entry which i parse using the yambo parser, i then reference this in a workflow archive something like ```{
    "workflow2":{
        "tasks": [
            {
                "m_def": "nomad.datamodel.metainfo.workflow.TaskReference",
                "name": "Task",
                "task": "/uploads/test_upload/entries/yambo_entry/archive#/workflow2"
              }
        ]
    }
}```
i use the ArchiveParser to parse this so make sure to name it something like workflow.archive.json
o no not necessary,  since i am not matching it
in your workflow file you can specify the Neb workflow in m_def and call the normalize function after parsing
Thanks a lot Alvin.

title: testing workflows
text: The first one `../upload/archive/mainfile/{mainfile}#/run/0` should work .

title: testing workflows
text:
It is possible that the mainfile is not correct, maybe have a look at the raw files under .volumes
I have just changed it to the reference with the mainfile. but now I have a connectionFailure: ```
 File "/home/jschumann/nomad-distro-dev-1/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/mongoengine/connection.py", line 419, in get_db
    conn = get_connection(alias)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/home/jschumann/nomad-distro-dev-1/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/mongoengine/connection.py", line 321, in get_connection
    raise ConnectionFailure(msg)
mongoengine.connection.ConnectionFailure: You have not defined a default connection```
you may have to run your infrastructure setup `docker-compose up ... nomad admin run worker`
for future reference, what was also missing was to write the archives like that ```upload_files.write_archive('{entry_id}', {archive}.m_to_dict())```
in case it helps someone in the future my python test script looks like this:
```python
from nomad.parsing.parser import ArchiveParser
from nomad.datamodel import EntryArchive, EntryMetadata
from nomad.datamodel.context import ServerContext
from nomad.files import StagingUploadFiles
from nomad.processing import Upload
import sys
from nomad.utils import get_logger

from atomisticparsers.utils import ASETrajParser

from nomad_neb_workflows.schema_packages.neb import NEBWorkflow
from nomad import infrastructure

infrastructure.setup()

logger = get_logger(__name__)

upload_files = StagingUploadFiles(upload_id='NEB_testdata', create=True)
upload = Upload(upload_id='NEB_testdata')
context = ServerContext(upload=upload)

ase_archive0 = EntryArchive(
    m_context=context,
    metadata=EntryMetadata(upload_id=upload.upload_id, entry_id='ase_entry0'),
)
ase_archive1 = EntryArchive(
    m_context=context,
    metadata=EntryMetadata(upload_id=upload.upload_id, entry_id='ase_entry1'),
)
ase_archive2 = EntryArchive(
    m_context=context,
    metadata=EntryMetadata(upload_id=upload.upload_id, entry_id='ase_entry2'),
)

ASETrajParser().parse(sys.argv[-3], ase_archive0, logger)
ASETrajParser().parse(sys.argv[-2], ase_archive1, logger)
ASETrajParser().parse(sys.argv[-1], ase_archive2, logger)

workflow_archive = EntryArchive(
    m_context=context,
    metadata=EntryMetadata(upload_id=upload.upload_id, entry_id='workflow_entry'),
)

ArchiveParser().parse(sys.argv[-4], workflow_archive, logger)

upload_files.write_archive('ase_entry0', ase_archive0.m_to_dict())
upload_files.write_archive('ase_entry1', ase_archive1.m_to_dict())
upload_files.write_archive('ase_entry2', ase_archive2.m_to_dict())
upload_files.write_archive('workflow_entry', workflow_archive.m_to_dict())

neb_workflow = workflow_archive.workflow2
neb_workflow.normalize(archive=workflow_archive, logger=logger)

import json
with open('output2.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(workflow_archive.m_to_dict(), f, indent=4)
```
Thanks @Alvin_Noe_Ladines for resolving🙏
